author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix(backtrace): tighten stdlib filter, add call-site display, cache getenv

Three deferred fixes from the adversarial review:

1. stdlib filter: replace ad-hoc substring search with canonical prefix
   from find_stdlib_dir() with basename fallback for AST-cached frames
   whose pos_fname comes from a different build directory.

2. call-site display: append "()" to plain identifier names in the
   interpreter backtrace so "panic() file:3" reads as a call site, not
   a definition site. Operators and <anon> are unchanged.

3. getenv cache: cache the MARCH_BACKTRACE env lookup in a static local
   on first call; also defer the stack-trace header in march_print_backtrace
   until at least one visible frame exists (avoids orphan header when all
   frames are stdlib-filtered).

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix(stdlib): harden Compress module against adversarial inputs

Critical C fixes:
- Add MAX_INPUT_SIZE (256 MB) and MAX_DECOMPRESS_SIZE (256 MB) constants to
  both march_compress.c and compress_stubs.c; gzip/deflate/zstd/brotli decode
  now check against the cap in every grow loop, preventing decompression bombs
- Fix zstd decode: check frame_size > MAX_DECOMPRESS_SIZE before malloc so a
  crafted frame header cannot cause a multi-GB allocation before any data is read
- Fix compress_bytes_to_raw: check malloc return value; all callers now handle
  the NULL return with a proper Err instead of passing NULL to zlib/zstd
- Add defensive input size check in all eight encode functions (four algorithms,
  two C files) so the uInt cast for zs.avail_in is always safe (no truncation)
- Copy OCaml String_val(input) to malloc buffer in all six stubs before any
  caml_alloc_* call; eliminates GC-move hazard on raw C pointer

March API fixes:
- Zstd.Fast(n): remove erroneous clamping of n < 1 to 1; zstd supports negative
  levels natively (e.g. Fast(-1) = ultra-fast); pass n through unchanged
- encode_stream/decode_stream (Gzip and Zstd): return
  Seq(Result(Bytes, Compress.Error)) instead of Seq(Bytes); errors are now
  surfaced per-chunk rather than silently dropped
- accept_encoding: split each token on ";" to strip q= parameters; exclude
  tokens with q=0 (client explicitly refuses); strips q=0.0, q=0.00 as well

New tests:
- deflate/zstd/brotli invalid decode → Err (were only tested for gzip)
- Zstd.Fast(-1) negative level compresses and round-trips correctly
- accept_encoding strips q= suffix ("gzip;q=0.9" → "gzip")
- accept_encoding excludes q=0 ("gzip;q=0, br" → ["br"])

author: Matthew Gilliam
message: docs(specs): update progress.md — interpreter backtraces implemented

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(backtraces): March call stack for interpreter panics

Adds three-layer panic improvements to the interpreter:

1. Call stack tracking — EApp expressions push a frame (fn name + file:line)
   before apply() and pop on normal return. Exceptions leave frames live so
   the top-level handler can print the full call stack.

2. Stdlib frame filtering — frames from stdlib/ paths are hidden by default;
   MARCH_BACKTRACE=full shows all frames.

3. Assert location — EAssert now includes file:line in the failure message.

4. Assert_failure handler — bin/main.ml now catches all three exception types
   (Eval_error, Match_failure, Assert_failure) and prints the backtrace.

5. C runtime frame table — march_frame_t linked list with march_frame_push /
   march_frame_pop / march_frame_reset for compiled binaries (Part 2 runtime).
   The struct and prototypes are in march_runtime.h; the implementation and
   march_panic integration are in march_runtime.c.

Plan doc updated to address review issues: threading clarification, String.starts_with
fix, #[no_backtrace] scoped to future work, default-on frames, TCO noinline note,
testing strategy, and EvalError coverage.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(stdlib): add Compress module — gzip, deflate, zstd, brotli

Implements stdlib/compress.march with sub-modules Gzip, Deflate, Zstd,
and Brotli.  Each sub-module provides one-shot encode/decode returning
Result(Bytes, Compress.Error), plus streaming encode_stream/decode_stream
variants.  HTTP helpers accept_encoding/best_encoding round out the API.

Implementation layers:
- runtime/march_compress.c: C shims over zlib (-lz, always available),
  libzstd (optional, auto-detected), libbrotli (optional, auto-detected).
  Missing optional libs return Err("not available") at runtime.
- lib/eval/compress_stubs.c: OCaml CAMLprim stubs (same logic) so the
  interpreter can call them via external declarations in eval.ml.
- lib/eval/dune: foreign_stubs + c_library_flags for the eval library.
- lib/eval/eval.ml: stdlib_gzip_encode/decode, stdlib_deflate_*,
  stdlib_zstd_*, stdlib_brotli_* builtins (Ok/Err wrapping Failure).
- lib/tir/llvm_emit.ml: name mappings + LLVM declare lines for all 8
  compress functions.
- bin/main.ml: march_compress.c included in both JIT .so and native
  clang invocations with auto-detected zstd/brotli flags.

Tests: 24 new tests in test_march.ml covering all algorithms, empty
input, invalid-data error paths, HTTP helpers, and 6 property tests
(Check.all with Gen.list(Gen.int(0, 255))) verifying encode∘decode = id
for all four algorithms.

Total: 1394 tests, all passing (was 1363 + 7 pre-existing = 1370; net
+24 compress tests, remaining delta from stdlib warm-cache).

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix(perceus): track TFn closures with RC + insert cross-branch EDecRC

Two interlocking bugs in lib/tir/perceus.ml caused a march_decrc crash
when Map.fold was called with a Bytes accumulator:

1. needs_rc (TFn _) = false — every function-typed parameter was
   invisible to Perceus. After defun, any AVar with TFn type is a
   heap-allocated closure struct (raw code pointers are ADefRef and
   never appear in AVar liveness). Setting this to false caused:
   - no EIncRC when f was captured into the go closure (EAlloc)
   - no EDecRC in the HEmpty arm where f is unused
   - the dangling closure slot was later misread as a Bytes payload
     pointer and freed via march_decrc, producing a .text-segment crash

   Fix: needs_rc (TFn _) = true. llvm_ty (TFn _) = "ptr" so EIncRC/
   EDecRC emit correctly at the LLVM level with no further changes.

2. ECase discarded live_before_br — variables live in some branches
   but dead in others never got EDecRC in the dead branches (a second
   class of RC leak). Fix: collect live_before_br per branch, compute
   union_live_br, insert EDecRC v at the head of each branch where
   v ∈ union_live_br but v ∉ live_before_br (excluding closure FVs,
   arm-bound vars, and vars live after the case).

   New _var_ctx global (Tir.var StringMap.t ref) provides correctly-
   typed Tir.var records for cross-branch EDecRC; seeded from fn params
   in insert_rc, extended via ELet with save/restore.

All 1358 tests pass.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(test): add Perceus TIR snapshot test framework (7 tests)

Add test/test_snapshots.ml with a snapshot framework that compiles minimal
March programs through the full pipeline (parse→desugar→typecheck→lower→
mono→defun→perceus) and diffs pretty-printed post-Perceus TIR against
.expected files in test/snapshots/perceus/.

Seven snapshots cover: FBIP reuse in recursive tree map, borrowed string
params (no EIncRC at extern call site), closure FV ownership (EIncRC before
EAlloc), scrutinee escape rewrite (xs-returning arm → EAlloc so FBIP fires),
actor atomic RC (EAtomicIncRC on first of two sends), RC elision pairs
(first peek gets EIncRC, last transfers ownership), and borrowed param at
last use (caller emits EDecRC, no EIncRC at callee site).

Regenerate with:
  UPDATE_SNAPSHOTS=1 SNAPSHOTS_DIR=$(pwd)/test/snapshots/perceus \
  dune runtest --force

Also add compiler instrumentation debug flags to specs/progress.md:
MARCH_DEBUG_PERCEUS, MARCH_DEBUG_BORROW, MARCH_SANITIZE, MARCH_DEBUG_RUNTIME,
and -g passthrough in dune dev C profile (landed in earlier commits).

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(debug): add compiler instrumentation for RC and borrow analysis

- dune dev profile: add -g to C stubs for debug symbols
- bin/main.ml: add -g to clang native/WASM compile when --debug is set;
  MARCH_DEBUG_RUNTIME=1 adds -g to the JIT runtime .so;
  MARCH_SANITIZE=1 adds -fsanitize=address,undefined to all clang paths
- lib/tir/perceus.ml: MARCH_DEBUG_PERCEUS=1 prints per-module RC stats
  (inserted inc/dec/atomic counts, cancelled pairs after elision, reuse count)
- lib/tir/borrow.ml: MARCH_DEBUG_BORROW=1 dumps per-function borrow map
  showing which parameters are inferred as borrowed vs owned

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
test(stdlib): add comprehensive property tests for String.from_codepoint and String.to_codepoints

Add 40+ unit and property-based tests covering:

Unit tests:
- Basic encoding of ASCII, 2-byte, 3-byte, 4-byte UTF-8 characters
- Invalid codepoint rejection (negative, out-of-range, surrogates)
- Basic to_codepoints on various strings
- Empty string handling

Property-based tests (using Check.all):
1. **Byte length properties**: Verify correct UTF-8 byte lengths
   - 1-byte for 0-127
   - 2-byte for 128-2047
   - 3-byte for 2048-65535 (excluding surrogates)
   - 4-byte for 65536-1114111

2. **Roundtrip properties**: encode → decode preserves original codepoint
   - Single codepoint roundtrip
   - Two-codepoint concatenation roundtrip
   - Three-codepoint concatenation roundtrip

3. **Invalid input rejection**: Surrogates, negative, out-of-range all return None

4. **Multi-codepoint extraction**: Verifies decoded codepoints are always valid

Tests use generated random codepoints to exercise the full valid Unicode range,
including edge cases at boundaries between UTF-8 encoding lengths.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(stdlib): add String.from_codepoint and String.to_codepoints

Implement proper Unicode codepoint encoding/decoding for UTF-8 support.

New builtins:
- string_from_codepoint(cp: int) -> Some(string) | None
  Encodes a single Unicode codepoint (0x0 to 0x10FFFF) as UTF-8.
  Validates range and rejects surrogates (0xD800-0xDFFF).
  Returns None for invalid codepoints.

- string_to_codepoints(s: string) -> list[int]
  Extracts all Unicode codepoints from a UTF-8 string as a list of ints.
  Handles 1-byte (ASCII), 2-byte, 3-byte, and 4-byte UTF-8 sequences.
  Invalid sequences are treated as individual byte values.

Stdlib functions:
- String.from_codepoint(cp: int) -> option[string]
  Wrapper around the builtin with proper docstring.

- String.to_codepoints(s: string) -> list[int]
  Wrapper around the builtin with proper docstring.

This fixes the issue in scroll's codepoint_to_utf8 where multi-byte UTF-8
sequences (e.g., é = 0xE9) were failing because Char.from_int was ASCII-only.

The interpreter now correctly encodes any valid Unicode codepoint as UTF-8.
For compiled code, llvm_emit.ml can call the C runtime function
march_codepoint_to_utf8 (added to march_extras.c for reference).

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix(runtime): pre-tag Int payloads in make_some_i64 and march_string_to_int

User-reported bug #34.

Root cause
==========

Under the uniform low-bit integer tagging scheme (commit 1696576),
polymorphic ptr-sized payloads storing an Int must be pre-tagged with
(n<<1)|1 so the compiler's `ashr #1` untag recovers the real value.

make_some_i64 and the analogous path in march_string_to_int stored
the raw int64_t in the payload.  The compiler, seeing Some(Int) /
Option(Int), emitted the untag shift; extraction halved the value:

    String.index_of("hello world", "world")
      interpreter:  Some(6)
      native:       Some(3)        <-- bug

    String.to_int("42")
      interpreter:  Ok(42)
      native:       Ok(21)         <-- bug

Presence checks that only distinguished Some(_) from None kept
working, which is why String.contains (bool-returning) was unaffected
and the bug stayed hidden.

Fix
===

make_some_i64 and march_string_to_int now pre-tag the payload
((int64_t)val << 1) | 1.  make_some_i64 is used by
march_string_index_of and march_string_last_index_of, both of which
are now correct without further changes.

Audited the other 24-byte alloc sites (make_some_ptr, mk_ok, file/
csv/actor helpers) — all pass pointer-typed values (or unit 0) that
do not need tagging.

Regression test
===============

New oracle test examples/rc_option_int_tagged_payload.march exercises
String.index_of and String.to_int.  Pre-fix native prints
'idx=3 / n=21'; post-fix prints 'idx=6 / n=42' matching the
interpreter.

dune runtest: 1359 OCaml tests — all green.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix(perceus/gc/runtime): four audit follow-ups (C1, C2, P1, M1)

C1 (runtime/march_runtime.c): march_decrc_freed now aborts on RC underflow
instead of silently double-freeing.  Mirrors march_decrc's existing guard.

C2 (runtime/march_gc.c): pass2_visit now uses GC_IS_HEAP_PTR which mirrors
the runtime's IS_HEAP_PTR (low-bit + sign-bit + <4096 guards).  Without
this, tagged-immediate integers from polymorphic containers (the new
uniform low-bit tagging scheme) were passed to fwd_lookup as candidate
pointers — wasted work and a latent corruption surface.

P1 (lib/tir/perceus.ml): post_dec_vars in EApp now dedups by v_name.
When the same heap variable is passed at multiple borrowed positions of
a single call (e.g. string_eq(s, s)) and is dead after, Perceus was
emitting one EDecRC per position, underflowing the caller's single
reference.  Confirmed regression: pre-fix the new
examples/rc_borrowed_dup_arg.march aborts with "RC underflow"; post-fix
it completes.

M1 (lib/tir/llvm_emit.ml): EReuse non-TCon branch now writes tag=0 in
the reuse path so both branches produce same-tagged cells (the fresh
branch goes through emit_heap_alloc which writes tag 0).

Tests:
- new test_gc_pass2_scalar_preservation in test/test_heap.c (7 PASS)
- new examples/rc_borrowed_dup_arg.march oracle program (P1 regression)
- additional non-pointer-input checks for march_decrc_freed in test_runtime.c

dune runtest: 1358 OCaml tests, 4176 C-runtime checks — all green.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(runtime/codegen): uniform low-bit integer tagging for polymorphic values

Fixes SIGSEGV when integers flow through polymorphic ADT fields (e.g.
List(Int) with n >= 4096).  The root cause: positive integers in [4096,
2^62] passed IS_HEAP_PTR and were dereferenced as march_hdr* in incrc/decrc.

Tag scheme: immediate scalars stored as (n << 1) | 1 (low bit = 1).
Heap pointers from march_alloc are always 8-byte aligned (low bit = 0).

Changes (Phases 1 + 2):

runtime/march_runtime.c
- IS_HEAP_PTR: add ((uintptr_t)(p) & 1u) == 0 as first clause so tagged
  immediates short-circuit before the page/sign guards on the hot RC path.
- march_value_to_string: check low bit before dereferencing; tagged
  immediates are formatted via march_int_to_string((intptr_t)p >> 1).

lib/tir/llvm_emit.ml
- coerce ("i64","ptr"): emit shl i64 %v,1 + or i64 %s,1 + inttoptr to tag.
- coerce ("ptr","i64"): emit ptrtoint + ashr i64 %i,1 to untag (sign-preserving).
- All three i64-returning closure wrapper emitters (top-level fn ref,
  AVar cross-module ref, REPL closure wrap) now tag the return value
  with shl+or before inttoptr.

test/test_march.ml
- test_int_tag_coerce_ir: verifies shl/or/ashr appear in IR for List(Int) code.
- test_int_tag_wrapper_ir: verifies wrapper emits tagged i64 return.

specs/{todos,progress}.md updated.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
perceus: fix double-dec_rc crash for let-rebinding shadows and branch vars

Two related RC underflow bugs that manifest when a borrowed parameter is
accessed through an ECase branch variable or when a function rebinds a
parameter name with let.

Fix A — borrowed-parameter shadowed by same-name let-binding:
  When json_escape(s) writes `let s = replace_all(s, …)`, lower.ml
  reuses the name "s" for the new binding.  Perceus's ELet case computes
  `live_for_rhs = live_into_body \ {v.name}`, removing the borrowed
  parameter "s" from live_after for the RHS.  post_dec_var then fires at
  the replace_all call site, inserting a spurious dec_rc for the borrowed
  parameter.
  Fix: in insert_rc, call rename_borrowed_shadows before RC insertion.
  This new pass renames only ELet/ECase-bound variables whose names collide
  with borrowed parameters, giving each shadow a fresh "s_b{N}" name so
  liveness analysis can distinguish parameter from binding.

Fix B — branch variables extracted from a borrowed scrutinee:
  In cell_to_json_obj, when the scrutinee (cell) is borrowed and the
  Code(s) branch extracts s, Perceus's ECase handling did not propagate
  the borrowed status to s.  After json_str(s) (a borrowing call), s
  appeared dead and post_dec_var inserted dec_rc(s) — a second decrement
  of the same string pointer that json_escape had already decremented.
  Fix: in the ECase branch handler, when the scrutinee is in live_after
  (i.e. it is borrowed), add all branch vars to la so post_dec_var cannot
  fire for them.

Also adds StringMap module to perceus.ml (needed by rename_borrowed_shadows).
All 1348 tests pass.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix(simplify): restrict 0/x → 0 to known non-zero literal divisors

The previous rule optimized `0 / x → 0` for any x, which is unsound
when x=0: it silently returns 0 instead of trapping with a division-
by-zero error. Now only fires when the divisor is a literal ≠ 0.

Updated test to verify variable divisors are left unchanged and added
a new test for the known-nonzero-literal case.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(session-types): TIR lowering for Chan.*/MPST.* — compile to native binaries

Wire session-typed channel operations through the full TIR pipeline
(lower → mono → defun → perceus → llvm_emit) so protocol-verified
code compiles to native binaries, not just the tree-walking interpreter.

- lower.ml: pattern-match Chan.new/send/recv/close/choose/offer and
  MPST.new/send/recv/close before general EApp, lowering to chan_*/mpst_*
  runtime calls
- llvm_emit.ml: builtin_ret_ty, mangle_extern, and LLVM declare entries
  for all 10 channel builtins
- runtime/march_extras.c: C runtime with mutex-protected queue pairs
  (binary) and N×N queue matrices (MPST); atomic refcounting for
  endpoint lifecycle
- 3 new tests in session_compile group (1317 total)

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(notebook): add server cells for long-running background processes

Extend the notebook server with a new cell kind — `\`\`\`march:server` fences
— that spawn background OS processes (HttpServers, actors, etc.) without
blocking the WebSocket event loop.

Implementation:
- Parse \`\`\`march:server fences as ServerCell(String) in Cell type
- generate_runner skips ServerCell items (they run independently)
- generate_server_runner wraps server cell source in mod/main
- start_server_proc: writes temp file, uses sh backgrounding
  ("march /tmp/... > /tmp/log &  echo $!") so Process.run returns immediately
- Server PID stored in Vault namespace for lifecycle management
- server_is_running: kill -0 check; stop_server_proc: kill -TERM
- read_server_log: reads /tmp log file on demand
- New WS messages: start_server, stop_server, read_server (server-side)
  and server_started, server_stopped, server_output, server_error (client-side)
- JS: server cell UI with green border, Start/Stop button, pid badge,
  scrollable output area, 2s polling via setInterval
- "+ Server" button in toolbar and between-cell add bars
- Cmd+Enter on server cells triggers Start

Also: rename Process.spawn → Process.spawn_async (spawn is a reserved keyword)

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(errors): Elm-like compiler errors with source rendering and suggestions

- Add render_diagnostic: shows source line with caret underline, Elm-style

- Improve cmp_op error to report types ("cannot compare a Int with a String")

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
perf(repl): add `march warm-cache` and run it during dune build

New `march warm-cache` subcommand pre-generates all REPL caches:
- stdlib_ast_<hash>.bin — parsed+desugared ASTs
- stdlib_tcenv_<hash>.bin — typecheck environment
- stdlib_prelude_O1_<hash>.so — JIT-compiled stdlib

Runs automatically as part of `dune build` via @all alias, so after
`dune build && dune install march`, the first `forge i` / `march repl`
starts with warm caches. Adds ~0.06s to incremental builds (cache hit)
and ~2.6s to clean builds (one-time stdlib .so compilation).

Also exports cache helper functions from repl.mli so they can be
called from main.ml.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
refactor: remove depot_* stdlib modules (moved to /code/depot)

The Depot.* in-memory/Vault-backed modules are now a standalone forge
library at /code/depot rather than part of march's stdlib. Apps that use
Depot should add it as a forge path dependency instead.

Removed from stdlib load order (bin/main.ml):
- depot_form.march, depot_gate.march, depot_schema.march
- depot_repo.march, depot_query.march, depot_migration.march
- depot_test.march

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix: stdlib module shadowing and stale resolver stdlib_module_names

Two bugs discovered when investigating Islands in interpreted/bastion mode:

1. stdlib/MARCH_LIB_PATH module shadowing (bin/main.ml)
   When a dep (e.g. bastion) provides a module that also ships in the
   stdlib (e.g. Islands, CSRF, Session), both versions were being loaded
   into mod_decls — the MARCH_LIB_PATH version silently shadowed the
   stdlib version, which could cause type confusion.
   Fix: after resolve_imports, collect top-level module names loaded from
   external paths and filter them out of stdlib_decls before prepending.
   The external version becomes the sole definition.

2. Stale stdlib_module_names in lib/resolver/resolver.ml
   The LSP and REPL resolver was missing ~20 stdlib modules introduced
   since the list was last updated (Islands, CSRF, Session, Vault, Test,
   Config, Crypto, all Depot.* variants, all Bastion* variants, etc.).
   Missing entries caused false "unknown module" diagnostics in the LSP.
   Fix: sync the list with what load_stdlib() actually loads.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(forge): compile @island modules to WASM during forge build

forge build now automatically detects modules annotated with @island,
compiles each to wasm32-unknown-unknown, and places the .wasm files in
the project's islands/ directory alongside the native binary.

Discovery: scans all .march files in lib/ for the @island annotation,
then extracts the module name from the following `mod Name do` line.
This matches the convention used by bastion.build.islands archive task.

Compilation: invokes march --compile --target wasm32-unknown-unknown
per island, reusing the same MARCH_LIB_PATH already built for the
native build so all deps resolve correctly.

Output: islands/<ModuleName>.wasm (e.g. islands/Counter.wasm).
Printed as "Islands: N/M compiled to islands/" after the native build.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
wip: fix LLVM codegen bugs #1-#7 for conduit forge build

Fixes applied to defun.ml, llvm_emit.ml, lower.ml, march_runtime.c:

1. defun.ml: Comprehensive builtin_names expansion (char, logger, int,
   float, TLS, actor, comparison, record, JSON, native array builtins)
   to prevent indirect-call rewrite of stdlib functions.

2. lower.ml: DLet handling in lower_mod_decls for top_lets collection;
   selective fn_body_uses injection replacing inject-into-all approach.

3. llvm_emit.ml (n_args_fallback): Use correct arity in ctor_entry lookup
   for switch arm tag computation instead of hardcoded 0.

4. llvm_emit.ml (switch deduplication): Deduplicate switch arms by tag
   before emission to prevent 'duplicate case value in switch' LLVM IR
   validation errors from dead TCO continuation blocks with wrong types.

5. llvm_emit.ml (polymorphic float arithmetic): Detect float operands in
   is_int_arith handler; emit fadd/fsub/fmul/fdiv for double operands
   instead of unconditional i64 integer ops.

6. march_runtime.c: Various runtime function additions needed by stdlib.

Remaining: '%base64_encode.addr' / '%random_bytes.addr' undefined value
errors in CSRF.generate_token and related Session/BastionCSP functions.
These occur because unqualified crypto function refs are not in top_level
(only qualified 'Crypto.base64_encode' is), so defun converts EApp to
ECallPtr requiring an alloca that doesn't exist.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
perf(resolver): deduplicate transitive imports to fix O(N²) typecheck cost

Root cause: resolve_imports was embedding the full DMod for each transitive
dependency *inside* every module that imported it. With conduit's 37 submodule
files all doing `import Conduit` (~80 declarations), the typechecker would
re-process the entire Conduit namespace 37 times, each time in a progressively
larger environment. This caused exponential compile-time growth: module #6 took
0.3s, module #50 took 13s, and the compiler hung on module #102 after 17+ mins.

Fix: after computing explicit_decls in step 1 of resolve_imports, recursively
mark all loaded module names in the `resolved` cache as `[]` (already-emitted).
When auto-discovered submodules call load_refs and encounter these names, they
return [] instead of the full DMod, so no re-embedding occurs.

Also remove debug timing/counter instrumentation added during profiling
(occurs step counter, discharge constraint logging, DMod inner-decl timing).

Result: conduit test app compiles in 0.3s (was 17+ minutes, ~3400x speedup).

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
perf(typecheck): replace O(n) association-list env with O(log n) StrMap

The typechecker environment used (string * _) list for vars, types,
ctors, and records fields. With 57 stdlib modules + transitive deps
merged into one flat module, every lookup scanned thousands of entries.
A minimal `import Conduit` app took 17+ minutes to typecheck.

Replace all four env fields with Map.Make(String).t for O(log n)
lookups. Use ctor_info list StrMap.t for ctors to preserve ambiguity
detection when multiple types define the same constructor name.

Add three regression tests (500-let module, 200-ctor type, 20x20
nested modules) with 2-second time bounds.

1314 tests pass.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix(forge): fall back to dep root when lib/ subdir doesn't exist

Path deps like `conduit = { path = "../conduit" }` only searched for a
lib/ subdirectory. If the dependency's march files live at the package
root (no lib/ dir), the dep was silently dropped from MARCH_LIB_PATH.

Add the same fallback that git_dep_lib_path uses: try dep/lib/ first,
then the dep root itself.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix(forge): restore git dep lib path resolution in build and run

Commit 7a9dc99 refactored GitDep into GitTagDep/GitBranchDep/GitRevDep
but replaced the working git_dep_lib_path call with | _ -> None, so
MARCH_LIB_PATH never included git dep lib dirs. Restore the handling in
both cmd_build.ml and cmd_run.ml so forge build and forge run both pass
git dependency paths to the march compiler/interpreter.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix: resolve 5 remaining REPL JIT test failures

Three root-cause bugs:

1. ptr 0 in LLVM string equality: when one operand is an i64 literal
   (e.g. bool false → "0"), the sprintf format produced `ptr 0` which
   is invalid LLVM IR. Fix: coerce both args to ptr before the call.

2. %List.length.addr undefined: polymorphic REPL globals have TVar "_"
   type, causing build_subst to return [] in the EApp branch of mono's
   rewrite_calls — the function was silently skipped and never emitted.
   Defun then converted the EApp to ECallPtr, producing an alloca load
   for a non-existent slot. Fix: add to worklist when subst=[] in EApp,
   matching the existing ECallPtr behavior.

3. Bool displayed as "1": when evaluating a REPL global with TVar "_"
   return type, Int64.to_string was used instead of the bool formatter.
   Fix: add global_tir_tys hashtable to repl_jit context; look up the
   actual TIR type in the TVar _ display branch via body inspection.

Also adds string_length to is_builtin_fn / mangle_extern (→
march_string_byte_length) / builtin_ret_ty in llvm_emit.ml — it was
registered in typecheck and defun but missing from LLVM emit, causing
@string_length undefined errors that were uncovered by fix #1.

All 1311 tests pass.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message: fix: formatter emits import instead of use for DUse declarations

author: Matthew Gilliam
message: feat: add --compiled flag to forge run

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix: resolve 14 pre-existing JIT/compiler-parity test failures

Root cause: run_expr/run_decl/precompile_stdlib received empty type_map
from tests. With no type info, ty_of_span returned TVar "_" for all
variable references during TIR lowering. TVar "_" caused build_subst
in mono to return [] for polymorphic calls (e.g. List.reverse inside
range_step), skipping specialization. The unspecialized function was
absent from tm_fns -> not in top_level -> defun treated it as a free
variable -> spurious closure capture -> invalid LLVM IR -> clang error.

Fix: run_expr, run_decl, and precompile_stdlib now compute type_map
internally via Typecheck.check_module m, ignoring the caller-supplied
~type_map. This ensures all expression spans have proper types before
TIR lowering, regardless of what the caller passes.

Also fixed jit_eval_simple_expr in tests to call check_module before
running the JIT, fixing the boolean display failure (1 == 1 → "true").

Reverted debug code that copied failing .ll files to /tmp/march_debug/.

Fixes: 10 repl_jit_regression + 3 repl_compiler_parity + 1 parity bool

author: Matthew Gilliam
message: fix: add unix to march_dump dune to silence OCaml 5.x deprecation warning

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat: support dotted names in impl, when constraints, and interface imports

Three related fixes for dotted module references:

1. Parser: `impl_decl` and `constraint_expr` now accept `upper_dot_path`
   instead of `upper_name`. Segments are joined with "." into the
   `impl_iface.txt` string, so `impl Conduit.Storage(T)` and
   `when Conduit.Storage(s)` parse correctly.

2. Typecheck `prebind_mod_members`: now also registers nested module
   interfaces as qualified names ("Conduit.Storage") alongside functions
   and types, so `impl Conduit.Storage(T)` resolves against the pre-bound
   interface.

3. Typecheck `DUse UseAll`: now imports interface names (not just vars)
   from the module prefix into scope with their short names, enabling the
   pattern `import Conduit` + `impl Storage(T)` to resolve correctly.

1 new parse test; 1311 total.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat: support dotted module paths in import declarations

Replace the four flat `import_decl` rules (each hard-coded to
`upper_name`) with a single rule plus a new right-recursive
`import_path_tail` nonterminal, modeled after the existing
`use_path_tail`. This enables:

  import Mod.Sub
  import Mod.Sub.{foo, bar}
  import Mod.Sub, only: [foo]
  import Mod.Sub, except: [foo]

The LALR(1) lookahead after each DOT is deterministic:
  DOT + LBRACE      → brace selector
  DOT + UPPER_IDENT → recurse (more path segments)
  COMMA             → only:/except: filter
  empty             → UseAll

Desugar/typecheck/eval already handle multi-segment use_path via
String.concat — no changes needed there.

3 new parse tests; 1310 total.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat: forge test uses compile pipeline; forge build excludes test files

- `forge test` now compiles tests via `march --compile --test` (same
  pipeline as `forge build`). Test binaries land in `.march/build/test/`.
  Falls back to interpreter when `MARCH_TEST_INTERPRETER=1` or `--coverage`.

- `forge build` explicitly excludes `test_*.march` / `*_test.march` files
  from the production binary scan so test code never enters release builds.

- `march --compile --test` flag: `DTest`/`DDescribe`/`DSetup`/`DSetupAll`
  blocks are lowered to zero-arg TIR functions (`__march_test_N__`,
  `__march_setup__`, `__march_setup_all__`). `tir_module` gains a
  `tm_tests : (string * string) list` field for test metadata.

- `llvm_emit.ml` emits a synthetic test-runner `@main` when `tm_tests`
  is non-empty: calls `march_test_init`, optionally `march_test_setup_all`,
  then `march_test_run` for each test, then `march_test_report`.

- `runtime/march_runtime.c/.h`: setjmp/longjmp test isolation harness
  (`march_test_init`, `march_test_run`, `march_test_setup_all`,
  `march_test_report`). `march_panic` longjmps when inside a test.

- Fix ctor_info suffix-search in `llvm_emit.ml` to resolve ambiguous
  constructor names by arity, fixing `Heap.HLeaf` (0 fields) vs
  `HEntry.HLeaf` (3 fields) collision that crashed compilation.

All 1302 existing tests pass.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat: allow multi-expression lambda bodies with let bindings

Lambda bodies after `->` now support leading `let`/`linear let` bindings
followed by a final expression, identical to match arm block bodies:

    fn x ->
      let y = x + 1
      let z = y * 2
      z

Implementation: new `lambda_body` / `lambda_stmts` grammar rules in
parser.mly. `lambda_stmts` greedily consumes leading LET tokens (which
cannot start an `expr`), so there are zero new parser conflicts. The
token filter is unchanged — NL suppression outside match contexts
already makes the token stream flat, which is exactly what the new
rules need.

Works for all lambda forms: `fn x`, `fn (a,b)`, `fn ()`, `fn _`.
Single-expression lambdas are unchanged (no EBlock wrapper added).

5 new parser tests; CLAUDE.md, syntax_reference.md, specs updated.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix: add string_chars/string_from_chars to LLVM codegen and C runtime

These builtins were defined in the typechecker and interpreter but
entirely absent from the compiled path, causing llvm_emit to fall
through to the default alloca-load path and generate undefined
%string_chars.addr references in LLVM IR.

- Add march_string_chars and march_string_from_chars to runtime/march_runtime.c
- Register both in llvm_emit.ml: is_builtin_fn, builtin_ret_ty, mangle_extern,
  and the LLVM preamble declare list
- Add regression tests (test_string_chars_llvm_emit) verifying the IR
  contains @march_string_chars and not %string_chars.addr

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix: add missing char/float builtins to LLVM codegen and runtime

- Add char_from_int, char_to_int, char_is_digit, float_to_int to is_builtin_fn
- Add corresponding mangle_extern mappings and builtin_ret_ty entries
- Add LLVM declare stmts for the new builtins to the module preamble
- Add march_char_from_int, march_char_to_int, march_char_is_digit,
  march_float_to_int C implementations to march_runtime.c
- Also add march_string_chars C implementation (from previous session)
- Rewrite stdlib/json.march to extract inner closures as top-level pfns,
  eliminating captured-builtin LLVM codegen errors

Remove from forge/lib/:

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix: allow keywords as lambda/fn parameter names

Reserved keywords like `state`, `init`, `loop` were lexed as dedicated
tokens (STATE, INIT, LOOP …), not as LOWER_IDENT, so `fn (state, event,
payload) -> …` produced a parse error at `state`.

Add `soft_lower_name` — a superset of `lower_name` that also accepts
12 commonly-used keywords in binding positions — and wire it into
`param`, `fn_param` (named-param alternatives), `simple_pattern`
(PatVar), and the single-name form of `lambda_params`.

Adds a regression test: `fn (state, event, payload) -> state` must
parse as a 3-param ELam with names ["state"; "event"; "payload"].

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix: typechecker support for default argument expansion

Previously the typechecker failed when encountering multiple DFn
declarations with the same name generated by expand_defaults_decl.
The 1-arg wrapper's self-reference got unified with the 2-arg function
type, causing an occurs-check failure, and call sites like greet("world")
got the wrong type (2-arg) because the last bind_var won.

Fix with four coordinated changes:

1. desugar: emit full-arity DFn first ([full_decl] @ extra_decls) so
   the typechecker processes it before any wrapper DFns.

2. check_fn: when the function name already has a concrete (non-TVar)
   type in env (from a previously-checked DFn with the same name), skip
   the self-reference shadow. The wrapper body sees the full-arity type
   and can call it without arity conflicts.

3. Pass 1 + check_decl: register each DFn under an arity-specific key
   ("name#N" in env.vars) in addition to the plain name. The full-arity
   binding wins for the plain name (not shadowed by wrappers).

4. EApp handler: when calling EVar fn with N args, first check for a
   concrete "fn#N" arity-specific scheme; if found, use that type
   instead of the plain name. This makes greet("world") resolve to the
   1-arg type (String -> String) and greet("world", "Hi") to the 2-arg
   type (String -> String -> String).

Also fix dune-project for dune 3.7 (depexts removal, lang bump) and
lib/cas/dune explicit modules field to resolve Module conflict.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix: default args TIR/LLVM support via name mangling

Default arg functions now generate uniquely-named DFns (greet$1, greet$2)
instead of same-named overloads that broke mono.ml's Hashtbl.replace.
eval.ml auto-registers base-name VMultiarity from $N patterns for arity
dispatch. lower.ml builds a _default_dispatch table and rewrites call
sites to mangled names. Also removes invalid depexts field from
dune-project and fixes lib/cas/dune module conflict.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix: resolve CI build failures (qcheck library rename + blake3 missing)

Two root causes:
- forge/test/dune referenced `qcheck` (renamed to `qcheck-core` since 0.18);
  also added qcheck-core/qcheck-alcotest as test-deps to forge.opam/dune-project
- lib/cas/dune hardcoded /opt/homebrew paths but blake3 was never installed on
  CI runners; replaced with a dune-configurator (discover.ml) that uses
  pkg-config with a platform-aware fallback; added dune-configurator dep and
  blake3 depexts to march.opam so `opam install --deps-only` installs it
  automatically (brew on macOS, apt on Ubuntu)
- Bumped CI Linux runner to ubuntu-24.04 where libblake3-dev is in apt repos

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix: stdlib modules (Crypto/UUID/Duration), Enum expansion, IO/System modules

- Crypto: add stdlib_sha256/sha512/random_bytes/base64_encode/base64_decode
  aliases in eval.ml to break infinite recursion (function shadowed builtin)
- UUID: fix pfn→fn for nested functions (pfn only valid at module level),
  fix missing `end` in set_version_variant, fix validate_format to
  short-circuit on length check before calling check_hex_range
- Duration: fix pfn→fn for `plural` nested inside `fn format`
- Enum: expand from 314→701 lines with chunk_every/zip/dedup/uniq/
  take_while/drop_while/sum/product/scan/with_index/intersperse/
  chunk_by/slide/frequencies/min_by/max_by and 17 new tests
- IO: new module (72 lines) — IO.puts/write/warn/read_line/gets/inspect
- System: new module (155 lines) — os/arch/cpu_count/monotonic_time/
  env/put_env/argv/cwd/pid/exit/cmd/version
- test_march.ml: fix `not b` → `!b` in secure_compare test (not without
  parens is a parse error in March)
- specs/todos.md, specs/progress.md: updated with all new modules

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat: add list comprehensions, with expressions, default args, opaque types

- List comprehensions: `[expr for pat in list]` and `[expr for pat in list,
  pred]` — parser-level desugar to List.map/filter (parser.mly)
- With expressions: Elixir-style `with Ok(x) <- f(), Ok(y) <- g(x) do x + y
  end` with optional else arms — desugars to nested EMatch (parser.mly)
- Default argument values: `fn greet(name, greeting \\ "Hello") do ... end` —
  FPDefault AST node, expand_defaults_decl in desugar.ml generates shortened
  DFn variants, VMultiarity in eval.ml dispatches by arity
- Opaque types: `opaque type Handle = Handle(Int)` — type name public,
  constructors private; opaque keyword in lexer+parser, var_vis=Private in
  typecheck

15 new tests (1233 total). syntax_reference.md updated with all 4 features.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat: compiler visualization Phases 5-10 — GC tracing, trace pipeline, viewers

Phase 5 — GC/RC runtime instrumentation:
- runtime/march_runtime.c: MARCH_TRACE_GC=1 enables per-event JSONL tracing
- Instruments march_alloc, march_free, march_incrc/decrc, march_incrc/decrc_local
- Each event: {event, addr, size, rc, tag, ts_ns} to trace/gc/gc.jsonl
- Lazy init (pthread_once), atexit flush, mutex-protected file writes
- march_process_argv_init registers atexit handler when tracing is on

Phase 6 — data pipeline:
- lib/dump/dump.ml: --dump-phases writes to trace/phases/phases.json
  (legacy march-phases/phases.json alias kept for compat)
- Emits trace/manifest.json listing available phase and GC trace data

Phase 7+8 — GC viewer (tools/gc-viewer.html):
- Canvas event-density timeline with per-type colour stacking
- Live-object count sparkline above event list
- Virtual-scroll event list (24px rows, handles millions of events)
- Per-event detail panel with full address history, clickable
- Filter buttons per event type, text/addr search
- Stats bar: total events, allocs, frees, peak live, leaked count

Phase 9 — optimization reasoning + ownership visualization:
- lib/dump/dump.ml: new count_tir_nodes, calls_self, inline_annotation
- TIR function metadata gains: inline_eligible, inline_reason, body_size,
  rc_density (rc_ops/body_size ratio)
- tools/phase-viewer.html: RC Density toggle recolours fn nodes on a
  blue→yellow→red heat scale; node info panel shows inline reasoning
  and rc_density bar

Phase 10 — trace validation (march analyze-trace):
- bin/main.ml: analyze_gc_trace function reads gc.jsonl
- Detects: leaked objects, double frees, negative reference counts
- Reports with address, RC, and event number for first 10 leaks
- Exits 0 if clean, 1 if any issues found
- Usage: MARCH_TRACE_GC=1 ./binary && march analyze-trace trace/gc/gc.jsonl

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat: add forge bastion gen json scaffolder

Generates a complete JSON API resource from a single command:
  forge bastion gen json Posts Post title:string body:text published:boolean

Creates 5 files:
- src/app/schemas/post.march   — Depot schema + gate + update_gate
- priv/depot/migrations/<ts>_create_posts.march — migration with up/down/version
- src/app/contexts/posts.march — CRUD context (list/get/get!/create/update/delete)
- src/app/handlers/post_handler.march — JSON handler using Conn.json/3 with all
  REST actions (index/show/create/update/delete) + not_found fallback
- test/handlers/post_handler_test.march — test stubs with describe/test blocks

Also prints REST route snippets to stdout for copy-pasting into the router.

Uses a {{PLACEHOLDER}} template engine (str_replace_all) instead of Printf.sprintf
to avoid arity-mismatch bugs in large format strings.

Also fixes pre-existing build errors in lib/repl/history.ml,
lib/repl/result_vars.ml, and lib/jit/repl_jit.ml (warning 69 unused-field
promoted to error prevented test_march.exe from building).

8 new tests in test/test_march.ml ("forge bastion gen json" suite).

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat: add LSP Performance Insights Phase 3 — async TIR pipeline and code lens

Runs `lower → mono → defun → known_call → perceus → escape` asynchronously
after each document open/change. Surfaces per-function TIR optimization data
as incremental publishDiagnostics and code lens annotations.

New types:
- `tir_fn_insight` — per-function counts of EStackAlloc/EReuse/ECallPtr/EAlloc
- `code_lens_item` — code lens annotation with range and title text
- `StackPromoted`, `FbipReuse`, `TirIndirectCall` variants of `perf_insight_kind`

New function `run_tir_pass`:
- Reparses source and runs the full TIR pipeline (guarded by try-with)
- Skips when the document has typecheck errors
- Produces per-function `tir_fn_insight` records and `code_lens_item` list
- Emits Hint-severity `perf_insight` entries at function definition spans

Server changes:
- `codeLensProvider` capability registered
- `on_notif_doc_did_open` / `on_notif_doc_did_change` spawn `Lwt.async` fibers
  that call `run_tir_pass`, update the doc cache, and push updated diagnostics
- `textDocument/codeLens` handler returns lens items (e.g. "⚡ 2 stack-allocated
  · ♻ 1 in-place · ⚠ 1 indirect call")

lsp/lib/dune: add `march_tir` dependency.

+6 LSP tests (147 total), 3 pre-existing failures unchanged.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat: add Task stdlib module — async/await/async_stream

Task.async(f), Task.await(task), Task.await_ms(task, ms),
Task.await_unwrap(task), Task.await_many(tasks), Task.await_many_ms,
Task.async_stream(list, f), Task.async_stream_n(list, f, max_concurrency).

Builds on task_spawn/task_await/task_await_unwrap interpreter primitives.
Zero-arg user functions are wrapped to the fn _ -> f() thunk signature.
max_concurrency and timeout params are advisory in Phase 1 single-threaded
scheduler, enforced in Phase 2+. Parses and typechecks with zero warnings.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat: add five new stdlib modules — Tuple, Char, OrderedMap, SortedSet, Range

- stdlib/tuple.march: 2-tuple utilities (first, second, swap, map_first,
  map_second, map_both, map_pair, make, to_list, from_list, size, all,
  any, fold, zip, unzip) — pure March using let-destructuring

- stdlib/char.march: character-level operations (is_alpha, is_digit,
  is_alphanumeric, is_upper, is_lower, is_whitespace, is_punctuation,
  to_upper, to_lower, to_int, from_int, to_string, equal, compare,
  is_ascii, is_printable) — thin wrappers over existing char_* builtins

- stdlib/ordered_map.march: AVL-tree sorted map (height-cached nodes,
  left/right rotations, rebalance) with explicit cmp comparator;
  put, get, get_exn, has, delete, keys (sorted), values, to_list,
  from_list, min, max, range, size, is_empty, map, filter, fold,
  merge, union — all O(log n)

- stdlib/sorted_set.march: AVL-tree sorted set with explicit cmp
  comparator; add, remove, member, to_list, from_list, min, max,
  range, size, is_empty, fold, filter, map, union, intersect,
  difference — all O(log n)

- stdlib/range.march: integer range as { start, stop, step }; new,
  new_step, to_list, size, is_empty, contains, map, filter, each,
  reduce, reverse, to_string; positive and negative steps supported

- bin/main.ml: register all five modules in stdlib load order
- lib/jit/dune, lib/repl/dune: suppress pre-existing warning 69
  (unused record fields) that became errors when building from
  worktree root with --root flag
- specs/todos.md, specs/progress.md: updated to reflect 1212 tests
  and document new modules

lowers its functions/types into the TIR output, completing the compiled
path for qualified module access (all 7 phases done).

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat: qualified module access Phase 6 — REPL tab completion

Enhanced the REPL tab completion engine to support qualified module
names. Typing "Map." now suggests all public exports (Map.get, Map.put,
etc.) by querying both scope entries and Module_registry.ensure_loaded.
Typing an uppercase prefix like "Ma" suggests "Map." as a module name.
Private members are excluded from suggestions.

3 new tests in repl_complete_qualified group (1208 total, 14 pre-existing).

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat: qualified module access Phase 4 — eval on-demand module loading

Add module_loader callback and ensure_module_loaded in eval.ml so that
qualified access like Map.get() triggers loading a stdlib module even
without explicit import. The lookup function and EField handler now call
ensure_module_loaded before failing for dotted names.

Added eval_stdlib_decls for evaluating DMod declarations into
module_registry without resetting global state. bin/main.ml wires the
callback before run_module using Module_registry.find_stdlib_file and
load_stdlib_file.

3 new tests in eval_qualified group (1205 total, 14 pre-existing).

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat: qualified module access Phase 3 — typecheck qualified resolution

Add on-demand qualified name resolution in the typechecker: when local
lookup fails for a dotted name like "Map.get", the typechecker splits
the name, loads the module via Module_registry.ensure_loaded, and
injects its exports into the env. Covers EVar (functions/values),
surface_ty TyCon (types), and ECon/PatCon (constructors).

Extended prebind_mod_members to pre-bind qualified types and constructors
from nested DMod declarations (previously only handled DFn).

Error messages use edit-distance suggestions for unknown modules,
unknown members, and private function access attempts.

6 new tests in typecheck_qualified group (1202 total, 14 pre-existing).

changed/2, apply_changes/1. All validators work with both v1 and v2 gates.
Register depot_gate.march + regex.march in stdlib loader and test runner.
97 tests passing (71 v1 + 26 v2).

Full design spec covering Schema, Gate v2, Repo, Query, Migration,

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix: forward-declare MARCH_FIELD/SET_TAG/mk_ok/mk_err_cstr before march_actor_call; tighten borrow test assertions

- runtime/march_runtime.c: move heap-layout #defines and static fn
  forward declarations above march_actor_call to fix C99 implicit-
  function-declaration errors introduced by the eager-elion merge
- test/test_march.ml: fix test_borrow_no_decrc_in_callee and
  test_borrow_conn_middleware_pattern to use wildcard Conn(_) patterns
  instead of Conn(s) + println(s) — the extern_borrow_table now
  correctly emits a post-call EDecRC for the borrowed-by-println string
  arg; tests should only check conn's own DecRC suppression
- specs/progress.md, specs/todos.md: update counts (1189 tests) and
  add cprop RC-target corruption fix to feature list

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix(borrow): add extern_borrow_table so Perceus doesn't leak RC for C string externs

borrow.ml's is_borrowed only knew about March-defined functions, so calls to
C externs like march_string_eq were treated as unknown callees — causing
borrow inference to mark their args as owned and Perceus to insert spurious
IncRC with no matching DecRC. Silent string RC leak on every comparison,
concat, or print in compiled mode.

Fix: add extern_borrow_table (hardcoded list of C extern and TIR builtin
names with per-parameter borrow flags) and is_extern_borrowed helper.
is_borrowed now falls back to is_extern_borrowed for callees absent from
the March borrow map. Covers:
  - march_string_eq/concat/byte_length/is_empty/to_int/to_float and all
    extended march_string_* ops (slice, split, replace, pad, etc.)
  - march_print/println and their TIR aliases (print, println)
  - march_compare_string, march_hash_string (used in synthetic Ord/Hash wrappers)
  - TIR builtin-name aliases (string_eq, ++, string_concat, etc.)

Build: dune build (clean). Tests: 1185 tests, 14 pre-existing failures (unchanged).

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(runtime+compiler): Actor.call/reply in compiled mode

Implements synchronous actor messaging (Actor.call/Actor.reply) for the
compiled LLVM path, which previously only worked in the eval interpreter.

C runtime (runtime/march_runtime.c):
- march_actor_call(actor, inner_msg, timeout_ms): reads the message tag
  from the zero-arg inner_msg, builds a new heap struct with the same tag
  and the calling green-thread proc pointer as field 0 (the reply channel),
  sends it to the actor's mailbox, then blocks via march_sched_recv().
  Returns Ok(result) or Err(reason).
- march_actor_reply(ref_ptr, result): casts ref_ptr back to march_proc*
  and delivers result via march_sched_send(), waking the blocked caller.

Protocol: actor handlers declare reply_to as first param (on GetCount(reply_to))
and call Actor.reply(reply_to, count). actor_call injects the reply channel
as field 0 of the sent message.

LLVM emit (lib/tir/llvm_emit.ml):
- actor_call, actor_reply, actor_cast added to is_builtin_fn
- Mapped to march_actor_call, march_actor_reply, march_send in builtin_name
- builtin_ret_ty entries: actor_call→Result ptr, actor_reply→Unit, actor_cast→Option
- LLVM declarations added to emit_preamble

Test: 1 new actor_compile IR test (actor_call/reply emitted).
1185 tests pass, 14 pre-existing REPL JIT failures unchanged.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix(cprop): prevent literal propagation into EDecRC/EIncRC/EFree targets

cprop was substituting literal-bound variables into the arguments of
EDecRC, EIncRC, EAtomicDecRC, EAtomicIncRC, and EFree. This turned
  let m = "POST" in ... DecRC(m)
into
  ... DecRC("POST")

LLVM emit would then allocate a fresh string object just to immediately
free it, while the original allocation leaked at RC=1.

Fix: leave RC and Free nodes unchanged in cprop — their argument must
remain the variable pointing to the original heap allocation.

Adds 3 regression tests: no_propagate_into_decrc, no_propagate_into_incrc,
no_propagate_into_free. 1188 tests total.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix(eval+typecheck): eval HTTP method atoms and actor_call type signature

Two bugs discovered while testing actor-backed HTTP servers:

1. eval.ml: http_method_of_string returned VCon("Get",[]) etc. (the old
   Http.Method variants) instead of VAtom "get". The atom routing fix
   (46ebff5) updated compiled-mode routing but missed the eval interpreter.
   HTTP method routing (:get, :post comparisons) now works in eval mode.

2. typecheck.ml: actor_call had signature `a -> a -> Int -> Result(a, e)`
   which unified the pid, message, and return type to the same type variable.
   Fixed to use 3 independent type variables: pid (any), msg (any), ret (any).

Tested with /tmp/counter_server.march (GET /count, POST /increment) and
bench/island_perf_server.march (POST /update) in both eval and compiled modes.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(compiler+runtime): Phase 5 GC/RC instrumentation + forge visualize

Runtime RC tracing (march_runtime.c):
- MARCH_RC_TRACE=1 env var emits JSONL events to march-phases/rc-trace.jsonl
- Instruments alloc, alloc_str, incrc, decrc, free, incrc_local, decrc_local
- Each event: op, ptr, rc_before/after, sz, seq number; mutex-serialised

TIR dump (lib/dump/dump.ml):
- New collect_rc_detail function: extracts ordered list of RC ops from TIR
- Adds rc_ops_detail to function node metadata: [{op, var, ty}] triples
- Covers EIncRC/EDecRC/EAtomicIncRC/EAtomicDecRC/EFree/EReuse with variable names

Phase viewer (tools/phase-viewer.html):
- GC Trace tab: loads rc-trace.jsonl, groups by object address
- Per-object lifecycle: alloc→incrc/decrc→free, collapsible event list
- UAF detection: objects with RC ops after free highlighted in red
- Node info renders rc_ops_detail as colour-coded chips (incrc=green, decrc=orange, free=red, reuse=blue)
- Auto-fetches /phases.json and /rc-trace.jsonl when served via forge

Compiler flag (bin/main.ml):
- --rc-trace: after native compile, runs binary with MARCH_RC_TRACE=1

Forge commands:
- forge build/run --rc-trace: passthrough flag
- forge phases: now also serves /rc-trace.jsonl
- forge visualize: new command — builds with --dump-phases then serves
  the phase viewer at http://localhost:4040 (forge/lib/cmd_visualize.ml)
  Supports --port, --rc-trace, --release flags

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(compiler+runtime): atom-based HTTP method routing — fixes compiled-mode POST/405 bug

HttpServer.Conn method field changed from Method ADT to Atom. Atoms compile
to FNV-1a 64-bit i64 hash constants in LLVM IR; the C runtime converts HTTP
method strings ("GET", "POST", …) to their lowercase atom hash at the TCP
boundary. This eliminates the Perceus RC leak on string method comparisons
that caused POST requests to always match the GET branch (405 responses).

Compiler changes:
- lib/tir/llvm_emit.ml: LitAtom → fnv1a_64 i64 constant; TCon("Atom",[]) → "i64"
  in llvm_ty/llvm_param_ty; atom switch cases use FNV-1a tag; is_ptr_scrut
  returns false for Atom scrutinees
- lib/tir/perceus.ml, borrow.ml: atoms excluded from needs_rc (i64 scalar, no heap)
- lib/typecheck/typecheck.ml: Atom added to Eq builtin_impls so == typechecks

Runtime changes:
- runtime/march_http.c: method_string_to_atom converts "GET"→fnv1a("get") etc.;
  make_conn takes int64_t method instead of void *

Stdlib changes:
- stdlib/http_server.march: Conn method field is Atom; method_to_string/1 added
- stdlib/websocket.march: removed local Method ADT, Conn uses Atom
- stdlib/csrf.march: protect/1 uses :get/:head/:options atom patterns
- stdlib/bastion_dev.march: uses HttpServer.method_to_string

All examples, bastion tests, and stdlib test files updated to use lowercase
atom method patterns (:get, :post, :put, :patch, :delete, :head, :options).

Also adds test_string_rc_bug.march: minimal reproducer documenting the
underlying Perceus RC leak on string comparisons (memory leak, not crash).

1185 tests pass.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(visualizer): pass-level instrumentation and stable node IDs (Phases 3-4)

Phase 3 — per-pass snapshots:
- lib/tir/opt.ml: add optional ~snap callback invoked after each individual
  optimizer pass (known-call, inline, cprop, fold, simplify, fusion, dce).
  Label format: tir-opt-{iter}-{pass}. Zero cost when snap is a no-op.
- bin/main.ml: wire ~snap into Opt.run when --dump-phases is on; emit one
  snapshot per pass instead of one coarse tir-opt snapshot.

Phase 4 — stable node IDs + diffing:
- lib/dump/dump.ml: switch from counter-based IDs (fn_foo_3) to deterministic
  name-based IDs (fn_<name>, type_<name>) so the same logical entity keeps
  its ID across every pass — enabling the diff viewer to correctly track what
  was added, removed, or modified. Added body_hash fingerprint (djb2 of
  show_expr output) to function metadata so body changes are visible in diff
  even when signature and call list are unchanged. Call edges now point to
  stable targets (tir_fn_id callee).

tools/phase-viewer.html:
- Opt passes grouped under "Opt iter N" section headers in sidebar.
- Per-pass color coding (known-call, inline, cprop, fold, simplify, fusion, dce).
- Diff summary bar in top bar: "+X added ~Y changed -Z removed" when diff
  mode is active.

specs: update todos.md and progress.md to reflect Phases 3-4 complete.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix(http): fix POST routing corruption and missing body in compiled mode

Two bugs in the evloop HTTP pipeline caused POST requests with bodies
to be misrouted as 405 Method Not Allowed:

1. march_http_evloop.c: The memmove() buffer shift ran BEFORE
   march_conn_from_parsed() was called. Since req->method/path/headers
   are raw pointers into c->rbuf, shifting by `consumed` (header bytes
   only) moved body bytes to position 0, turning "POST" into "1OST".
   Fix: move memmove to AFTER the for loop that builds all conns.
   Also extend `consumed` to include Content-Length body bytes so the
   full request is removed from the buffer on each iteration.

2. march_http.c march_conn_from_parsed(): Body was always set to ""
   regardless of Content-Length. Fix: scan request headers for
   content-length and read that many bytes from req->method + header_end.

Verified: method_test.march routes GET→hit:GET, POST (no body)→hit:POST,
POST (with body "1")→hit:POST. counter_server.march compiled mode:
5 increments → count=5, decrement 2 → count=3.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message: docs: update specs for actor+HTTP scheduler integration fix

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix(runtime): integrate actor scheduler with HTTP event loop

Two bugs prevented actors from working in compiled mode with HttpServer:

1. **Scheduler never ran**: march_http_server_listen() blocks forever in
   the kqueue/epoll event loop. The @main wrapper calls march_run_scheduler()
   after march_main() returns — but it never returns when serving HTTP.
   Actors were spawned (green threads queued) but nothing dispatched them.

   Fix: march_spawn() now calls march_ensure_sched_started() which launches
   a background pthread running march_sched_run(). This runs alongside the
   HTTP event loop threads. For non-HTTP programs, march_run_scheduler()
   joins the background thread instead of running inline — no behaviour
   change for the non-HTTP path.

2. **actor_get_int field index mismatch**: march_actor_get_int(actor, 0)
   read word 0 of the actor struct (= rc = 1) instead of the first state
   field. The eval interpreter correctly treats index 0 as "first state
   field", but the C runtime was reading the raw word index.

   The actor struct layout has a 4-word header before state fields:
     [0] rc  [1] tag+pad  [2] $dispatch  [3] $alive  [4+] state fields

   Fix: add offset 4 in march_actor_get_int so index 0 → word 4 (first
   state field), matching eval behaviour.

Reproducer: counter_server.march — actor counter stayed 0 in compiled
mode because messages queued but never dispatched, and actor_get_int
read rc (=1) instead of count (=0..N).

   The actor struct layout has a 4-word header before state fields:
     [0] rc  [1] tag+pad  [2] $dispatch  [3] $alive  [4+] state fields

   Fix: add offset 4 in march_actor_get_int so index 0 → word 4 (first
   state field), matching eval behaviour.

Reproducer: counter_server.march — actor counter stayed 0 in compiled
mode because messages queued but never dispatched, and actor_get_int
read rc (=1) instead of count (=0..N).

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix(vault): make Vault.update atomic — single lock hold for read-apply-write

The previous implementation released the shard lock between reading the
current value and writing the updated value (3-phase: lock/read/unlock,
apply, lock/write/unlock). This created a window where a concurrent writer
could overwrite the key, and then the update would silently clobber it.

Fix: hold the shard lock for the entire operation — read, apply f, write —
so no concurrent writer can interleave. The 3-phase commit check (re-read
before writing) is removed; since we never drop the lock, it is unnecessary.

Trade-off: f must not call vault operations on the same shard (same key hash
bucket) or it will deadlock. Cross-shard and cross-table calls remain safe.
All 13 vault tests pass; the 14 pre-existing repl_jit failures are unrelated.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(compiler): --dump-phases flag serializes each IR stage to JSON for visualization

march --dump-phases --compile file.march writes march-phases/phases.json with
a graph representation of every pipeline stage (parse through tir-opt). Each
phase captures nodes (functions, types, actors) with metadata (RC op counts,
call lists, arity) and edges (call graph). forge build/run --dump-phases thread
the flag through.

New: lib/dump/dump.ml (JSON serializers for AST+TIR), tools/phase-viewer.html
(Cytoscape.js visualizer with diff mode, phase selector, node info panel).

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(bench): state concurrency test suite — actor/Vault/island findings

Three new test files investigating concurrent state mutations:

  bench/state_actor_test.march   — eval-mode actor correctness (7 tests, all pass)
  bench/state_vault_test.march   — eval-mode Vault semantics (4 tests, all pass)
  bench/state_conc_server.march  — compiled HTTP server (actor endpoints, port 8900)
  bench/state_conc_test.mjs      — Node.js harness documenting all findings

Key findings:

1. Compiled actors + HttpServer.listen() = actors never process messages.
   HttpServer uses kqueue/epoll event-loop threads. Actors need
   march_sched_run() on the green-thread scheduler. These systems never
   intersect — messages enqueue but no scheduler runs to dispatch them.
   Fix: start scheduler threads alongside the HTTP server.

2. actor_get_int index mismatch between eval and compiled mode.
   Eval:     index 0 = count (0th field of state record)
   Compiled: index 4 = count ([0]=rc [1]=tag [2]=dispatch [3]=alive [4+]=state)
   The previous island_perf_server used index 0 in compiled → returned rc,
   not count (hence count always appeared 1).

3. Vault has no native C implementation — eval-only.
   Vault.set is atomic (lock-replace-unlock, no lost writes).
   Vault.update is non-atomic (read-unlock-apply-lock-write — can lose
   updates under true concurrency, safe in single-threaded eval mode).

4. No island conflict detection mechanism exists at any layer.
   Islands are stateless WASM transforms. No CRDTs, OT, or version vectors.
   Mitigation: route all mutations through a single actor mailbox.

Also adds `Bastion.Components.error_tag/2` for rendering field errors in
templates, and fixes pre-existing parse errors in the Bastion.Static

message:

BastionCookies — put_signed/get_signed (HMAC-SHA256) and put_encrypted/get_encrypted
(authenticated encryption) with PBKDF2 key derivation from secret_key_base.
Cookie-name-scoped key contexts prevent cross-cookie forgery.

BastionRoutes — Vault-backed route registry. register/4, all/0, find_by_name/1,
path/3 (fills :param slots + appends percent-encoded query strings), path1/2, path2/3.

BastionTelemetry — attach/detach/execute API, TelemetryEvent type with
name/measurements/metadata, Vault-backed ring buffer (1000-entry cap), Aggregator
sub-module (500-entry cap), register_before_send/register_after_send Conn hooks.

BastionHotDeploy — DrainStatus FSM (Serving/Draining/Starting/Stopped), DrainConfig,
Vault-backed state, in-flight request counter, health_status/1 JSON, drain_response/1,
should_drain_request/1.

BastionCSP — Per-request nonce via assign_nonce (random_bytes(16) + base64), strict
default CSP policy with nonce injection, protect/1, protect_with_overrides/2,
report_only/2, disable/1.

BastionPubSub — Named instances, Adapter = Local | Redis(String) | Cluster,
subscribe/unsubscribe/broadcast/broadcast_from/local_broadcast_sync/subscribers,
wildcard topic matching, Vault-backed subscription and broadcast log.

BastionTestSandbox — SandboxEnv (id, vault_namespace, pubsub_name), checkout/release,
checkout_tracked/release_tracked, is_active/active_count, isolated Vault namespace
(vault_put/vault_get/vault_delete), build_conn/3, build_conn_full/5.

BastionIdempotency — IdempotencyState = InProgress(Int) | Completed(...),
get_key/1 (reads Idempotency-Key header), lookup/mark_in_progress/complete_entry,
replay_response/2, invalidate/3, protect/3 handler wrapper. 5xx not cached.
X-Idempotent-Replayed header. 409 on concurrent duplicates.

dune build clean.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(lsp): Performance Insights Phase 1 — TCO, actor send copy, closure captures

Three AST-level analysis passes in lsp/lib/analysis.ml:

1. NonTailCall (Warning, code: non_tail_call)
   tco_check walks each DFn body tracking tail position with a
   `blocking: string option` context. Emits a Warning when a
   self-recursive call is not in tail position, names the blocking
   operation (operator, constructor, etc.), and suggests the
   accumulator pattern. Works on the raw AST — no TIR required.

2. ActorSendCopy (Warning, code: actor_send_copy)
   send_copy_check detects ESend nodes whose message argument has a
   complex non-linear type in type_map (List, record, custom TCon,
   tuple). Suggests declaring the argument `linear` for zero-copy
   ownership transfer.

3. ClosureCapture (Hint, code: closure_capture)
   lambda_free_vars computes the free variable set of a lambda body
   relative to its own parameters. closure_capture_check emits a
   Hint when a lambda closes over ≥3 values, lists the captured
   names, and suggests grouping them into a record.

Infrastructure:
- perf_insight_kind variant + perf_insight record added to analysis.ml
- perf_insights : perf_insight list field added to Analysis.t
- All three passes wired into diagnostics (publishDiagnostics)
- perf_insight_at hover query added; server.ml hover shows insight
  message alongside type and doc-string sections

11 new LSP tests covering: non-tail call detection, tail-recursive
not-flagged, constructor-wrapped non-tail, warning in diagnostics,
non-recursive not flagged, large/small closure detection, hint in
diagnostics, capture count accuracy, actor send smoke tests, and
perf_insight_at hover integration.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix(typecheck): extend module load-order independence to typecheck pass

Pass 1 of check_module/check_module_full now pre-binds all public
qualified names (e.g. "B.greet") from nested DMod declarations as
fresh forward-reference type variables, mirroring the eval-time
module_registry fix already in eval.ml.

Previously the eval fix allowed cross-module forward references at
runtime but the typecheck pass would still reject them with "I cannot
find a variable named B.greet". Now the full pipeline (typecheck +
eval) handles sibling modules referencing each other regardless of
definition order.

Private (pfn) functions and private modules are intentionally excluded
from pre-binding to preserve access restrictions.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat: multi-file project-aware compilation for Bastion apps

Scaffolded Bastion apps now compile end-to-end. Three changes to
resolve_imports in bin/main.ml:

1. module_name_to_filename now handles dotted names:
   - MyApp.Router -> my_app/router.march
   - MyApp.Templates.Layout -> my_app/templates/layout.march

2. loaded_paths hashtable tracks loaded file paths so the same file
   is never parsed twice when explicit `use` and auto-discovery overlap.

3. Auto-discovery: after resolving explicit imports, all .march files
   in MARCH_LIB_PATH dirs are loaded. Files are sorted by module-name
   dot-depth (more dots = deeper namespace = fewer dependents = load
   first) so the evaluator sees dependencies before their users:
   Templates.Page.Index -> Templates.Layout -> PageController -> Router

Validated: full Router.dispatch -> PageController.index ->
Templates.Layout.wrap -> Templates.Page.Index.render chain works on
a forge-bastion-new scaffolded test_app_01 with MARCH_LIB_PATH set.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message: fix: WASM island pipeline end-to-end validation Four bugs found and fixed: 1. find_fn in island export codegen matched Vault.update instead of the user module's update function — narrowed suffix matching to exact (bare name) or user module prefix only. 2. Vault builtins (vault_new/get/set/update/drop/size/keys/whereis/ set_ttl) were absent from is_builtin_fn, mangle_extern, builtin_ret_ty, and the LLVM preamble declarations. Generated broken IR: `%vault_update.addr` undefined (treated as free closure variable). 3. ECallPtr to a known builtin (e.g. vault_update called from Vault.update) was not short-circuited to a direct call — added a guard case before the general ECallPtr/closure dispatch path. 4. build_wasm.sh compiled march_runtime.c (native, needs ucontext.h/ pthreads) instead of march_runtime_wasm.c; also targeted wasm64-wasi instead of wasm32-unknown-unknown. march_runtime_wasm.c additions: - memset/memcpy/memmove stubs (compiler generates these at -O2 with -nostdlib; no libc available in wasm32-unknown-unknown) - vault_* stubs that __builtin_trap() — vault is native-only Validation: 9/9 JS WebAssembly.instantiate tests pass: march_island_init, march_island_render, march_island_render_html, march_island_update, march_alloc_export, march_dealloc, march_island_string_length, march_island_string_data, string content "hello from wasm" confirmed correct. Note: march_island_render_html works for IOList-returning renders. String-returning renders should use march_island_render + march_island_string_{length,data} directly.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat: CorrelationId middleware — structured logging with request correlation IDs

- Add `uuid_v4` builtin to eval.ml: RFC 4122 v4 UUID (version nibble=4,
  variant bits=10xx), 36-char hyphenated hex string

- New `stdlib/correlation.march` — `CorrelationId` module:
  - `assign(conn)` middleware: reads X-Request-ID header (≤200 bytes) or
    generates a UUID v4; stores in conn.assigns["request_id"]; echoes as
    X-Request-ID response header; injects into Logger context via
    `Logger.with_context` so all subsequent log calls in the request
    process automatically include `request_id=<id>` — no manual threading
  - `request_id(conn)` accessor: returns ID from assigns or ""
  - `clear()`: resets Logger context for process reuse

- 15 tests in `test/stdlib/test_correlation.march` covering uuid_v4 format,
  header acceptance, oversized-header rejection, response echo, and Logger
  context injection

- Registered `correlation.march` in `test_stdlib_march.ml` stdlib load list
  and test suite (19 stdlib_march tests, all passing)

- Updated specs/todos.md (item marked ✅) and specs/progress.md

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat: CorrelationId middleware — structured logging with request correlation IDs

- Add `uuid_v4` builtin to eval.ml: RFC 4122 v4 UUID (version nibble=4,
  variant bits=10xx), 36-char hyphenated hex string

- New `stdlib/correlation.march` — `CorrelationId` module:
  - `assign(conn)` middleware: reads X-Request-ID header (≤200 bytes) or
    generates a UUID v4; stores in conn.assigns["request_id"]; echoes as
    X-Request-ID response header; injects into Logger context via
    `Logger.with_context` so all subsequent log calls in the request
    process automatically include `request_id=<id>` — no manual threading
  - `request_id(conn)` accessor: returns ID from assigns or ""
  - `clear()`: resets Logger context for process reuse

- 15 tests in `test/stdlib/test_correlation.march` covering uuid_v4 format,
  header acceptance, oversized-header rejection, response echo, and Logger
  context injection

- Registered `correlation.march` in `test_stdlib_march.ml` stdlib load list
  and test suite (19 stdlib_march tests, all passing)

- Updated specs/todos.md (item marked ✅) and specs/progress.md

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat: Bastion adversarial test suite — 346 tests, stdlib/islands.march bug fix

Adds a comprehensive adversarial test suite for the Bastion web framework
stdlib modules in examples/bastion_tests/. All 346 tests pass.

Test files added:
- test_routing.march (42): run_pipeline, halt semantics, method dispatch,
  path params, response helpers, Bastion.Depot.with_pool
- test_caching.march (37): ETag/304, Bastion.Cache.cached/fragment/invalidate/
  invalidate_prefix, cache_control/no_cache/public_cache helpers
- test_sessions_adversarial.march (35): edge-case values (=, ;, unicode,
  spaces), overwrite semantics, flash, Vault backend, multi-cookie headers
- test_csrf_adversarial.march (44): body parsing edge cases, base64 token
  chars, duplicate token fields, all mutating methods, JSON exemption,
  skip+protect interaction, pipeline integration
- test_dashboard.march (64): request_timer, conn_inspector, live_reload_script,
  inject_live_reload, live_reload_handler, error_overlay, dashboard_html,
  inc_ws/dec_ws
- test_html_adversarial.march (54): XSS vectors, Unicode, double-escaping,
  ~H sigil interpolation, Html.tag attribute injection, layout title XSS,
  Html.list/render_collection, content_hash
- test_islands.march (70): hydrate_attr, wrap output structure, state JSON
  escaping, CSS attribute escaping, wrap_with_css, wrap_eager, client_only,
  bootstrap_script, preload_hint, Registry, Descriptor accessors, Html integration

Bug fix — stdlib/islands.march:
  wrap_with_css and wrap_eager_with_css were defined twice; the first definition
  used a weak escape_css_attr helper (only escaped "). Removed the dead first
  definitions and unused escape_css_attr. The surviving second definitions use
  escape_attr_value which correctly escapes &, ", <, >.

Also adds examples/bastion_tests/RESULTS.md documenting all findings.

No regressions in existing stdlib tests (test_csrf, test_session, test_html,
test_bastion_dev all pass unchanged).

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat: forge bastion command group (new, server, routes)

- forge bastion new APP_NAME: scaffolds a full Bastion web app with
  config/{config,dev,test,prod}.march, router, controllers, templates,
  static assets, and tests. Immediately runnable via forge bastion server.

- forge bastion server: starts the March interpreter with MARCH_ENV=dev,
  prints a banner with URL + dashboard link, then polls lib/ + config/
  mtimes every 1s. On change, SIGTERMs child and forks a fresh server;
  BastionDev SSE reconnect triggers browser reload. SIGINT exits cleanly.

- forge bastion routes: scans lib/<name>/router.march for ROUTE comments
  and (:method, [...]) match-arm patterns; prints a formatted table.

New files: scaffold_bastion.ml, cmd_bastion_{new,server,routes}.ml
Tests: forge/test/test_bastion.ml (20 tests, all passing)

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat: BastionIdempotency middleware — idempotency keys for Bastion

Implements the Stripe-style idempotency keys pattern as a stdlib
middleware module. For POST/PUT/PATCH requests, reads the
Idempotency-Key request header and caches the full response
(status code, headers, body) in Vault.

Behaviour:
  - Cache miss:      marks key in-flight, runs handler, caches Done entry
  - In-flight entry: returns 409 Conflict immediately (concurrent duplicate)
  - Done entry:      replays cached status + headers + body verbatim

GET/DELETE/HEAD and requests without the key header pass through
unchanged. Default TTL is 24 hours; configurable via with_ttl/1.
Custom header name supported via with_key_header/1.

New files:
  stdlib/bastion_idempotency.march  — BastionIdempotency module
  test/test_march.ml                — 15 new tests (all passing)
  specs/todos.md                    — item marked done
  specs/progress.md                 — test count + feature entry updated
