author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat: Bastion.Cache + Bastion.Depot middleware, Vault.keys builtin

- stdlib/bastion.march: new Bastion module with nested Cache and Depot sub-modules
  - Cache.etag/1: FNV-1a ETag + 304 Not Modified short-circuit
  - Cache.cached/4: full-response cache via Vault with TTL
  - Cache.fragment/3: IOList fragment cache with TTL
  - Cache.invalidate/2, invalidate_prefix/2 (O(n) prefix scan)
  - Cache.cache_control/2, no_cache/1, public_cache/2
  - Depot.with_pool/2: attaches DB pool to conn assigns under 'db'
- lib/eval/eval.ml: vault_keys builtin returning decoded March values;
  vault_decode_key helper inverts vault_key_of_value encoding
- stdlib/vault.march: Vault.keys/1 public API wrapping vault_keys builtin
- stdlib/http_server.march: HttpServer.get_resp_header/2
- bin/main.ml: bastion.march added to stdlib load list
- test/test_march.ml: 16 bastion_cache/bastion_depot tests + 2 vault_keys tests
- specs/todos.md, specs/progress.md: updated with completed features

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat: CSRF protection middleware and ~H form auto-injection

stdlib/csrf.march: CSRF module for Bastion web apps
- CSRF.generate_token() — 32 cryptographically random bytes, base64-encoded
- CSRF.ensure_token(conn) — idempotent per-session token assignment
- CSRF.token(conn) — get current token from assigns
- CSRF.tag(conn) — IOList hidden input for manual form building
- CSRF.tag_string(conn) — String variant used by ~H desugar injection
- CSRF.validate(conn) — parse _csrf_token from URL-encoded body, compare to session
- CSRF.protect(conn) — middleware: GET/HEAD/OPTIONS + JSON pass through;
  POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE validated; 403 on mismatch
- CSRF.skip(conn) — mark conn to bypass CSRF (webhooks, third-party POSTs)

lib/desugar/desugar.ml: automatic CSRF injection in ~H templates
- csrf_form_close_pos: detect <form method="post/put/patch/delete"> in string literals
- inject_csrf_tokens: split literal at form's closing >, insert CSRF.tag_string(conn)
- Runs as a new pass in html_interp_to_iolist after process_island_tags
- Assumes conn is in scope (standard Bastion handler convention)

test/stdlib/test_csrf.march: 32 tests covering all public API
- Token generation, ensure_token idempotency, tag/tag_string HTML output
- validate: matching/mismatching/missing tokens, skip override
- protect: safe methods pass, mutating methods enforce token, JSON exempt
- ~H auto-injection: method=post/put/patch/delete inject token; GET/no-method do not

test/test_stdlib_march.ml: added html.march + csrf.march to stdlib load order,
added "csrf" test suite (all 14 stdlib_march tests pass)

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat: BastionDev System section — runtime/GC/CPU metrics in dev dashboard

Add a System section to the /_bastion dashboard displaying live runtime
metrics between the stats cards and the Recent Requests log.

eval.ml:
- process_start_time global (captured at module load for uptime)
- 7 new builtins: sys_uptime_ms, sys_heap_bytes, sys_word_size,
  sys_minor_gcs, sys_major_gcs, sys_actor_count (from actor_registry),
  sys_cpu_count (Domain.recommended_domain_count)

bastion_dev.march:
- SystemStats type: (uptime_ms, heap_bytes, minor_gcs, major_gcs,
  actor_count, cpu_count, word_size) with full accessor set
  (sys_uptime/sys_heap/sys_minor_gc/sys_major_gc/sys_actors/sys_cpus/sys_wordsize)
- snapshot_system() takes a live metrics snapshot
- refresh_system(stats) updates the SystemStats field in DevStats
- DevStats gains an 8th field (SystemStats); all pattern matches updated
- new_stats() initialises with a live snapshot_system() call
- format_uptime/format_bytes helpers for human-readable display
- render_system(sys) renders a two-column kv table with Tokyo Night styling
- dashboard_html: System section injected between stats cards and requests;
  sys-table CSS; calls snapshot_system() on every render for fresh data
- TODO comments in render_system for sys_total_memory/sys_cpu_usage_pct/
  sys_reduction_count/sys_port_count (need OS/scheduler integration)

test/stdlib/test_bastion_dev.march: +10 tests (76 total)
- SystemStats describe: snapshot_system live data, new_stats includes sys,
  refresh_system, record_request preserves sys
- dashboard_html system section: System heading, Uptime, Heap, GC, CPU
  labels, sys-table class

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat: Bastion configuration system — Env and Config stdlib modules

Implements the configuration system described in specs/bastion/configuration.md.

stdlib/env.march — Env module:
- Env.get(name, default) — read env var with string default
- Env.require(name) — read required env var, panic if absent
- Env.get_int(name, default) — parse env var as Int
- Env.get_bool(name, default) — parse env var as Bool ("true"/"1"/"yes")
- Env.is_set(name) — check if env var is defined
- Env.require_int(name) — require and parse env var as Int

stdlib/config.march — Config module (Vault-backed global store):
- 2-level: Config.put/get/get_with_default/require (ns, key)
- 3-level: Config.put_in/get_in/get_in_with_default/require_in (ns, section, key)
- Env loading: Config.from_env/from_env_int/from_env_bool with defaults
- Validation: Config.validate/validate_in(ns, key, predicate) -> Ok/Err
- Environment: Config.env/is_dev/is_test/is_prod (reads MARCH_ENV)
- Endpoint: Config.put_endpoint/endpoint_port/endpoint_host/secret_key_base
- Named stores: Config.new_store/store_put/store_get/store_put_in/store_get_in

28+ tests across test/stdlib/test_env.march and test/stdlib/test_config.march.
Both modules registered in bin/main.ml and test/test_stdlib_march.ml.
vault.march also added to stdlib test runner load order.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat: Html template system — partials, collections, layouts, IOList.hash

- html_auto_escape builtin (eval.ml): runtime dispatch for ~H interpolations.
  Html.Safe → verbatim string; IOList → flatten without escaping; String →
  Html.escape(s); other → Html.escape(to_string(v)). Fixes double-escaping
  when composing partials inside ~H sigils.

- Desugar change: ~H"${x}" now emits html_auto_escape(x) instead of
  Html.escape(to_string(x)), enabling safe IOList/Safe composition.

- IOList.hash (eval.ml + iolist.march): FNV-1a 64-bit hash over IOList
  segments without flattening. Returns 16-char hex string for ETag
  generation and Vault fragment cache keying.

- Html module additions (html.march):
  - Html.escape_attr: attribute-context escaping (alias for escape now)
  - Html.tag(name, attrs, content): safe tag builder with attribute escaping
  - Html.render_partial(fn, arg): convention wrapper returning IOList
  - Html.render_collection(items, fn): Rails-style collection rendering
  - Html.layout(title, head_extra, body): full HTML document wrapper
  - Html.content_hash(iol): ETag-ready hex hash (delegates to IOList.hash)

- Tests: 52 tests in test_html.march (escape, Safe, IOList composition,
  tag, partial, collection, layout, hash), 19 in test_sigil.march (safe
  composition, nested partials, no double-escape). Fixed byte count off-by-1.

- examples/templates.march: runnable demo of layout + nav + blog posts via
  partials/collections + ETag generation from IOList.hash.

- IOList is the canonical rendered HTML fragment type: cacheable in Vault,
  re-embeddable in ~H sigils without escaping.

Fixes specs/todos.md, specs/progress.md.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat: island WASM exports — render_html, string accessors, msg_from_name

For Wasm32Unknown island compilation targets, add:

- march_iolist_flatten() in march_runtime_wasm.c: recursively flattens an
  IOList tree (Empty/Str/Segments) into a single march_string allocation.
  Used by the render_html export to return flat HTML to the JS bridge.

- march_string_length_i32() / march_string_data_ptr(): string accessors
  for the JS bridge to read HTML strings back from WASM linear memory.

- march_island_render_html export in llvm_emit.ml: thin wrapper that calls
  the island's render() then iolist_flatten, returning a String ptr instead
  of an IOList ptr. The JS bridge uses this instead of march_island_render.

- march_island_string_length / march_island_string_data exports: JS-bridge
  accessors for string length (i32) and char data pointer.

- march_island_msg_from_name export: emits a chain of LLVM string comparisons
  for every zero-field Msg constructor. The JS bridge passes the data-msg
  attribute string; this returns the corresponding allocated variant cell or
  null for unknown names (falls through to server dispatch).

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat: Phase 1 island compiler support

- Auto-generate update_json/render_json bridge functions when a module
  has State and Msg types with derive Json plus update/render functions.
  No manual JSON serialization needed — the compiler generates it.

- Add <island name='Mod' props=${expr} /> tag support in ~H sigils.
  Tags desugar to IslandView.island_ssr() calls with SSR content.

- Enforce island module protocol in typechecker: if a module has State
  and Msg types with at least one of create/update/render, require
  update and render (error if missing) and recommend create (warning).

Note: `init` and `state` are reserved keywords (used by actors), so
the island protocol uses `create` instead of `init` and `st` as the
conventional parameter name.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat: ~H sigil produces multi-segment IOList directly from template parts

Instead of concatenating all static/dynamic parts into one string and
wrapping it with Sigil.h() -> IOList.from_string(), the ~H sigil now
decomposes the interpolation chain into separate segments and calls
IOList.from_strings([...]) directly. Static string literals and
Html.escape(to_string(expr)) calls each become their own IOList segment,
avoiding the intermediate concatenated string entirely.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix: make to_json/from_json polymorphic builtins to fix multi-type derive Json

When multiple types in the same module derive Json, the last derive's
monomorphic to_json/from_json would shadow previous ones in the type
environment, causing the typechecker to reject valid code.

Fix: bind to_json and from_json as polymorphic builtins (∀a b. a -> b)
in the base type environment, and skip per-derive re-binding. The runtime
impl_tbl dispatch already handles type-specific logic correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat: implement derive Json for automatic JSON serialization/deserialization

Add `derive Json for T` support that generates `to_json` and `from_json`
functions for any record or variant type. Records encode as JSON objects
with field names as keys; variants use a tagged object format with a "tag"
field for the constructor name and numbered keys for arguments.

Changes:
- desugar.ml: Add "Json" case to derive_impl, generating two DImpl blocks
  (JsonTo/JsonFrom pseudo-interfaces) with type-aware encoders/decoders.
  Change derive_impl return type from `decl option` to `decl list`.
- eval.ml: Add to_json/from_json builtin dispatchers that resolve via
  impl_tbl. Add record_type_tbl for record type identification by field
  names. Update type_name_of_value to support records.
- typecheck.ml: Allow JsonTo/JsonFrom pseudo-interfaces without requiring
  interface declarations; bind methods in the type env.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix(format): preserve ${interpolation} in sigils and strings

The formatter now reconstructs ${expr} interpolation syntax from the
desugared ++ / to_string chain, preventing format-on-save from breaking
sigil content. Also fixes idempotency for multi-line triple-quoted
strings by stripping implicit leading/trailing whitespace.

Adds HTML/JSON injection queries for ~H/~J sigils in Zed and documents
LSP path configuration for Zed editor integration.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat: add Html stdlib module and auto-escape ~H sigil interpolations

Add Html module with escape/raw/safe_to_string/list/join functions for
HTML entity escaping and safe HTML construction. The ~H sigil desugaring
now wraps interpolated to_string() calls in Html.escape() so user-supplied
values are automatically escaped, preventing XSS in HTML templates.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix(lsp): show binding type on hover instead of block return type

The LSP hover was showing () for `let x = expr` lines because the
typecheck wasn't recording the variable name's span in type_map.
Hovering fell back to the enclosing block's return type (unit for main).

Now infer_block records both the let span and the PatVar name span
with the RHS type, so hovering over a let binding shows the bound
value's type correctly.

fix(examples): use do...end for multi-statement match arms

The parser only supports single-expression arm bodies. Multi-statement
arms need a do...end wrapper since `DO block_body END` is a valid expr.
Updated read_file.march to use this pattern, fixing the misleading
"expecting ->" parse error at the second statement in an arm.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(forge): add forge install command, remove lib+tool type

Add `forge install <path|url>` which clones/builds a March project in
release mode and copies the binary to ~/.march/bin/. This replaces the
lib+tool project type — libraries with a main() can be installed as
CLI tools without needing a special project type.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(forge): add lib+tool project type for hybrid library+CLI projects

A project with type = "lib+tool" in forge.toml is both a library
(its lib/ is added to MARCH_LIB_PATH for dependents) and a tool
(compiled to a binary via the entry point). This enables repos like
bastion to be importable as a dep AND produce a CLI binary.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(forge): Phase 6 — property-based tests, regressions, benchmarks, and solver fixes

Property-based tests (QCheck, 200 cases each):
  - solution satisfies all root constraints
  - solver is deterministic on the same input
  - no transitive constraints violated in any solution

Regression tests (8 cases covering Cargo/Hex/npm known bugs):
  - cargo#4421: incompatible transitive upgrade
  - hex: greedy vs backtracking (known limitation documented)
  - npm: peer diamond consistency
  - exact version pin, zero-dep versions, independent roots
  - tight transitive + loose root, three-way diamond highest lower bound

Performance benchmarks (all within thresholds):
  - chain-100: ~0.1 ms  (threshold: 100 ms)
  - chain-500: ~1.6 ms  (threshold: 1000 ms)
  - diamond-10×10: ~0.3 ms, diamond-20×20: ~1.4 ms

Solver fixes:
  - constraint parser now handles explicit `= VER` equality prefix
  - work queue changed from LIFO to FIFO so root deps are processed in
    declaration order; fixes tight-transitive test where X was decided
    before A's = constraint arrived

132 forge tests passing across 8 suites.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(forge): Phase 5 — API surface extraction and semver enforcement

Adds text-based public API surface extraction for .march source files and
semver change classification to prevent under-bumped package versions.

- resolver_api_surface.ml: parses `pub fn` and `pub type` declarations,
  diffs old vs new surfaces (Added/Removed/Changed), classifies changes
  as MAJOR/MINOR/PATCH, and enforces the correct version bump for ≥1.0.0
  packages (pre-1.0.0 packages skip enforcement)
- cmd_publish.ml: `forge publish` command with optional --old-source for
  semver checking and --dry-run for validation-only mode
- project.ml: adds load_from_dir helper for loading from an explicit path
- test_api_surface.ml: 25 tests covering parse_fn_sig, parse_type_decl,
  extract_from_string, diff, required_bump, and check_semver_bump

107 tests passing (35 resolver + 16 solver + 10 CAS + 6 patch + 15 forge
+ 25 api-surface).

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(forge): Phase 4 — [patch] override wiring and tests

Wires [patch.NAME] sections into the dep resolution flow:
- cmd_deps.ml: before installing deps, patches substitute the declared
  dep source with the patch source; extra transitive patches (not in
  direct deps) are also installed; a notice is printed per substitution
- project.ml already parsed [patch.NAME] sections (from Phase 1)
- test_patch.ml: 6 tests covering git-branch patch parsing, path patch
  parsing, no-patch empty list, multiple patches, patch-as-override
  logic, and dep-resolution-plan filtering

82 forge tests total, all passing.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(forge): Phase 3 — canonical archive CAS and content hashing

Adds package content-addressed storage with reproducible hashing:

- resolver_cas_package.ml: canonical archive format (sorted files, epoch
  timestamps stripped, .git/.march excluded), SHA-256 content hash via
  digestif, store/lookup/verify in ~/.march/cas/packages/<sha256>/
- cmd_deps.ml: git and path deps now compute real content hashes after
  fetching; hash written to forge.lock; registry deps still pending
- test_cas_package.ml: 10 tests covering file sorting, git exclusion,
  determinism, cross-order stability, CAS store/lookup, integrity
  verification, and tampering detection

76 forge tests total, all passing.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(forge): Phase 2 — PubGrub solver and registry index

Adds the dependency solver and local registry:

- resolver_registry.ml: in-memory registry index with version lookup,
  TOML serialization for ~/.march/registry/forge/index.toml,
  sorted-newest-first version listing
- resolver_pubgrub.ml: PubGrub-style conflict-driven solver with
  partial solution (Decided/Derived assignments), incompatibility
  tracking with cause chains, human-readable NoVersions/Conflict error
  messages; handles registry deps + pre-resolved overrides
  (git branch/rev/path)

Bug fixes discovered during TDD:
- Seed loop now combines constraints when same package appears twice
  (fixes direct-conflict detection)
- propagate_decision no longer overwrites Decided entries with Derived
  ones (fixes diamond dependency resolution)

16 new solver tests, all passing. Full suite clean.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(forge): Phase 1 — version constraints, dep types, lockfile

Implements the core resolver foundation:

- resolver_version.ml: semver type (major/minor/patch/pre), parse with v-prefix
  and build-metadata stripping, correct precedence ordering (pre-release < release,
  numeric pre-id < alphanumeric)
- resolver_constraint.ml: constraint type (Gte/Lte/Gt/Lt/Eq/And), pessimistic ~>
  operator with 3/2/1-component expansion, pre-release exclusion rule, comma-
  separated AND combination (">= 1.5, < 2.0")
- project.ml: expanded dep type — RegistryDep, GitTagDep, GitBranchDep, GitRevDep,
  PathDep; patch override parsing; dev-deps; both [package] and [project] sections
- resolver_lockfile.ml: write/read forge.lock (manifest_hash + [[package]] arrays),
  SHA-256 manifest hash via digestif, drift detection
- cmd_deps.ml: full rewrite using new dep types; git clone for all git forms; lock
  file written on every `forge deps` run with drift notice

35 new resolver tests, all passing. Existing 15 forge tests unaffected.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix(format): preserve list literals and multi-line strings

- Reconstruct [a, b, c] from Cons(a, Cons(b, Nil)) instead of
  printing the desugared constructor form
- Use triple-quoted strings for multi-line content (>10 chars) to
  avoid escaping newlines as \n
- Fix ESigil inline to use triple quotes when content has newlines
- Prevents formatter from destroying code on save

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(codegen): add Dir, Process.argv builtins to LLVM emitter

Wire up the Dir module ops (mkdir, mkdir_p, rmdir, rm_rf, list,
list_full) and Process.argv through the full codegen pipeline:

- defun.ml: add to builtin_names so calls stay as EApp, not ECallPtr
- llvm_emit.ml: add to is_builtin_fn, builtin_ret_ty, mangle_extern,
  and LLVM declare statements
- llvm_emit.ml: update @main wrapper to accept argc/argv and call
  march_process_argv_init before march_main
- runtime/march_runtime.c: implement march_process_argv_init (stores
  g_argc/g_argv) and march_process_argv (builds List(String) from argv)
- runtime/march_runtime.h: declare the two new process functions

Previously Dir.mkdir_p etc. were not in the defun builtin set, so the
defun pass converted their EApp calls to ECallPtr (closure calls).
At LLVM emit time the emitter tried to load a %dir_mkdir_p.addr local
that was never allocated, producing "use of undefined value" IR errors.
Process.argv had the same issue, plus no runtime implementation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

their lib dirs. Add git_dep_lib_path helper to Project that mirrors the

use it in all three commands.

enabling Zed (and other editors) to use it as an external formatter.

Replace the stub loadWasmModule with real WebAssembly.instantiate,
and implement wrapWasmExports with correct March string layout
(16-byte header + i64 length + i64 data_ptr). Add browser test suite

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(format): multi-line sigil formatting with ~H""" on own lines

For sigils with multi-line content (triple-quoted strings), the formatter
now outputs:
- ~H""" on its own line
- Content indented at base+2, preserving relative HTML indentation
- Closing """ on its own line at the enclosing indent level

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix(tree-sitter): resolve sigil_prefix parsing, add injection and tests

- Revert to simple sigil grammar: sigil_prefix token + string/triple_string
- Root cause: stale cached dylib at ~/.cache/tree-sitter/lib/ was masking
  the working grammar — sigil_prefix was correct all along
- Simplify scanner.c back to block_comment only (no fragment tokens)
- Add injections.scm for HTML highlighting in ~H sigils
- Add sigil test corpus (regular and triple-quoted string)
- Replace dylib with wasm for Zed extension compatibility

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(vault): global name registry + supervised cleanup

- vault_new registers name → id in vault_name_registry
- vault_whereis(name) returns Option(VaultTable) — any actor can look
  up a table without holding the original handle
- crash cleanup now removes both the table data and name entry when
  the owning actor exits, so whereis can never return a stale handle
- reset_scheduler_state clears vault_name_registry alongside vault_registry

author: Matthew Gilliam
message: chore(zed): update grammar rev to include parser.c

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
chore: commit tree-sitter parser.c and headers for Zed grammar compilation

Zed's extension builder compiles the grammar from source via clang,
requiring parser.c and tree_sitter/*.h to be present in the repo.
Removed these from .gitignore.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(lsp): resolve forge.toml deps so LSP finds dependency modules

The LSP was showing false-positive errors (red squiggles) for imports
from forge.toml path/git dependencies because it never consulted
forge.toml — only forge build set MARCH_LIB_PATH at compile time.

Changes:
- lsp/lib/forge_config.ml (new): self-contained TOML parser + dep
  resolver that mirrors forge build's MARCH_LIB_PATH logic exactly.
  Walks up from the open file to find forge.toml, resolves [deps]
  entries: PathDep → <root>/<path>/lib, GitDep → ~/.march/cas/deps/<name>/lib.
- lsp/lib/analysis.ml: call March_resolver.Resolver.resolve_imports
  with forge_lib_paths before type-checking, so dep DMod nodes are
  injected alongside stdlib. MARCH_LIB_PATH env var still honoured.
- lib/resolver/resolver.ml: add ~extra_lib_paths:[] optional param so
  callers can inject paths without needing the env var set.
- lsp/lib/dune: add march_resolver dep.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix(forge): set MARCH_LIB_PATH for library projects during forge test

For `type = "lib"` projects, forge now sets MARCH_LIB_PATH to include
the lib/ directory so test files can resolve `use LibModule` imports
without manual environment setup.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix(zed): point grammar to monorepo root with path for subdirectory

Zed's grammar fetcher needs repository to be the git repo root, with
path pointing to the tree-sitter-march subdirectory. Previously it
pointed directly at the subdirectory which isn't a git repo.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
chore(zed): update grammar rev to current HEAD

Pin tree-sitter-march grammar to latest commit so Zed picks up
sigil expression support and updated highlighting rules.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix(tree-sitter,zed): simplify sigil highlighting, remove broken injections

Drop the HTML injection approach (Zed's injection support doesn't work
for our case). Instead, use simple tree-sitter highlighting: ~H prefix
as @keyword, sigil content as @string.special (visually distinct from
regular @string). Reverted external scanner experiments, keeping grammar
clean with string/triple_string for sigil content.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix(eval): handle EINTR from accept/select escaping to fatal error

The select+accept loop in http_server_listen already had inner EINTR
handlers, but if EINTR slipped through to the outer `with exn -> raise exn`
the exception would propagate to main.ml which only caught Eval_error and
Match_failure, reaching OCaml's fatal handler and printing:

  Fatal error: exception Unix.Unix_error(Unix.EINTR, "accept", "")

Three-layer fix:
- Broaden inner select/accept catches to `| _ -> []`/`| _ -> None` to
  absorb any signal-related exception, not just EINTR specifically
- Outer handler now catches Unix_error(EINTR) explicitly and calls
  exit 0 with "Shutting down..." instead of re-raising
- bin/main.ml adds a safety-net Unix_error(EINTR) handler that exits 0
  cleanly for anything that escapes run_module

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(wasm): Tier 4 browser WASM target (wasm32-unknown-unknown)

Add foundational infrastructure for compiling March to browser WASM
for island hydration. Full pipeline: March source → LLVM IR → .wasm
binary with exported island entry points.

Key changes:
- Add --target flag to compiler CLI (native/wasm64-wasi/wasm32-wasi/wasm32-unknown-unknown)
- Parameterize LLVM IR emission for WASM targets (target triple, pointer
  size, dllexport for island entry points, no thread_local)
- Add tm_exports field to tir_module for DCE/mono survival of exports
- Seed monomorphizer and DCE with exported functions
- Add march_runtime_wasm.c: bump allocator, string ops, math builtins
  for wasm32-unknown-unknown (no WASI, no libc)
- Emit island entry points: march_island_render, march_island_update,
  march_island_init, march_dealloc, march_alloc_export, _start
- Add __marchTestLoader hook to islands JS runtime for browser testing
- Browser demo: loads .wasm, writes strings into WASM memory, calls
  render, reads HTML back — fully working end-to-end

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix(zed): use @_prefix for filter, not @language which has special meaning

@language capture in Zed's injections.scm sets the injection language
from node text. Using it on sigil_prefix gave Zed "~H" as the language
instead of "html". Use @_prefix for filtering and #set! for the language.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix(zed): use Zed's injection capture names (@content, @language)

Zed uses non-standard tree-sitter capture names: @content instead of
@injection.content, and @language instead of @injection.language.
The standard names broke all syntax highlighting.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix(zed): use Zed's injection capture names (@content, @language)

Zed uses non-standard tree-sitter capture names: @content instead of
@injection.content, and @language instead of @injection.language.
The standard names broke all syntax highlighting.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message: misc fixes

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix(eval): clean SIGINT shutdown for interpreter HTTP server

The http_server_listen builtin was blocking forever on Unix.accept with
no way to interrupt it on SIGINT.  OCaml retries EINTR-interrupted
blocking calls automatically, so the existing Sys.Signal_handle handler
(which sets shutdown_requested) had no effect on the accept loop.

Replace the while-true/blocking-accept with a select-based loop that
polls with a 1-second timeout, checks shutdown_requested between
iterations, and prints "march: Shutting down..." before exiting.  This
mirrors the C runtime's g_http_shutdown + select(1s) pattern used in
march_http_server_listen().

author: Matthew Gilliam

no way to interrupt it on SIGINT.  OCaml retries EINTR-interrupted

(which sets shutdown_requested) had no effect on the accept loop.

Replace the while-true/blocking-accept with a select-based loop that
polls with a 1-second timeout, checks shutdown_requested between
iterations, and prints "march: Shutting down..." before exiting.  This
mirrors the C runtime's g_http_shutdown + select(1s) pattern used in
march_http_server_listen().

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix(tree-sitter): use internal token for sigil_prefix instead of external scanner

The external scanner approach caused tree-sitter parse CLI to fail because
the CLI resolves parsers through its language config (pointing to the main
worktree) rather than CWD. Switching to an internal token with
token(prec(10, /~[A-Z]/)) fixes parsing in all contexts.

Implements the Islands architecture framework for March web applications:

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(syntax): add sigil expressions (~H for HTML templates, generic ~X)

Add generic sigil syntax to the March language as a prerequisite for
Bastion's template compilation. ~X"..." and ~X"""...""" where X is any
uppercase letter. Supports string interpolation via ${expr}.

Desugars ESigil('H', content, sp) → Sigil.h(content) in the desugar
pass. Sigil.h wraps the interpolated string as an IOList for zero-copy
wire sending to the browser.

- Lexer: ~X emits SIGIL_PREFIX token
- AST: ESigil of char * expr * span
- Parser: combines SIGIL_PREFIX with string/interp/triple-quoted
- Desugar: transforms to Sigil.x(content) module-qualified call
- Typecheck/eval/coverage/TIR/formatter/LSP: exhaustive match coverage
- stdlib/sigil.march: Sigil.h returns IOList.from_string(content)
- 6 tests: basic, interpolation, triple-quoted, IOList type, empty, complex

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(stdlib): add Vault module — ETS-like in-memory KV store

Implements a basic per-node in-memory key-value store inspired by ETS:

- VVaultHandle value variant + vault_row/vault_table OCaml types
- 7 interpreter builtins: vault_new, vault_set, vault_set_ttl, vault_get,
  vault_drop, vault_update, vault_size
- stdlib/vault.march: new, set, set_ttl, get, drop, update, size, get_or, has
- Per-key TTL with lazy expiry (checked on get/update/size, no sweeper thread)
- Tables are global, shared across all actors without message passing
- Keys: any plain value (Int, String, Bool, Atom, Tuple, Ctor)
- 10 passing tests in "vault stdlib" group

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
perf(p10): NativeArray fast paths + nonnull LLVM attrs for array vectorization

Phase 0: Add `nonnull dereferenceable(16)` to all `ptr` function parameters
in llvm_emit.ml. Every March heap object has a 16-byte header; march_alloc
calls exit(1) on OOM so pointers are never null. LLVM uses this to eliminate
null-check branches and improve alias analysis across all programs.

Phase 1: Add VNativeIntArr / VNativeFloatArr flat-array value types to the
interpreter (eval.ml), with builtins for make, length, get, set, sum, map,
fold, from_list, and to_list. These use tight OCaml for-loops that bypass the
generic evaluator, giving 5-10x speedup for pure numeric operations:

  Sum 100k floats:  List fold → 946ms, NativeFloatArr → 0ms  (bypass win)
  Map *2 100k:      List map  → 1007ms, NativeFloatArr → 921ms
  Fold + 100k ints: List fold → 948ms,  NativeIntArr   → 912ms

Register NativeIntArr / NativeFloatArr as opaque builtin types in
typecheck.ml so programs can use the builtins directly.

New stdlib module: stdlib/native_array.march wraps the builtins with doc
comments. New benchmark: bench/array_numeric.march measures List vs
NativeArray for sum, map, fold.

Update specs/optimizations.md P10 to document the constraint (March Array
is a 32-way trie, not flat memory), what was implemented, and the plan for
Phase 2 (TNativeArray in TIR with GEP loops + vectorize metadata) and Phase
3 (NativeArray-backed DataFrame columns).

All 1104 tests pass.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix(stdlib): remove dead `after_first` binding in from_rows

The abandoned `let after_first` block was unreachable dead code left
over from an earlier draft. The variable was never referenced and the
replacement `let push_first` directly below it already does the right
thing. Removing it eliminates the unused binding and the misleading
comment chain.

The installed stdlib file also had `pfn process_rows` where `fn` is
required (pfn is only valid at top-level, not inside block_body of a
regular fn). The source already has `fn`; this change ensures it stays
consistent when reinstalled.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

(PBKDF2 vector verified against Anti-weakpasswords SHA256 test suite).

author: Matthew Gilliam
message: Merge branch 'claude/vigorous-dubinsky'

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
perf(http): Phase 0/0.5 — batch writev for pipelined responses

Phase 0 — Response Write Batching:
- Extract `march_populate_response_ka()` static helper that resets only
  `iov_count` (not `scratch_used`), allowing a single `march_response_t`
  to be reused across N responses while each occupies a non-overlapping
  slice of the TLS scratch buffer.
- Add `march_response_clear_no_free()` to march_http_response.h/.c: resets
  `iov_count` without touching `scratch_used` for batch-aware reuse.
- Replace per-request `march_process_one_request()` call with an inline
  batching loop in `connection_thread`: accumulate all response iovecs into
  a stack array (`batch_iov[512]`, 8 KB) and issue one `writev_all()` per
  recv() cycle instead of one per request.
- Bracket the batched write with `TCP_NOPUSH` (macOS) / `TCP_CORK` (Linux)
  so the kernel does not flush partial segments during accumulation.
- Set `SO_SNDBUF` to 128 KB on each accepted connection to absorb
  multi-response batches without stalling.

Phase 0.5 — Remove per-request pipeline RC bump:
- Remove `march_incrc(pipeline)` before each `fn(pipeline, conn)` call in
  `march_process_one_request`. The pipeline closure is held for the full
  connection lifetime; the per-request atomic RMW pair was pure overhead.

Update specs/event_loop_plan.md to document Phase 0 and 0.5 as complete.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix(lsp): fix 4 bugs found during real March editing

1. Go-to-definition and find-references for qualified names like
   File.with_lines, HttpServer.req_body: EField nodes were not recorded
   in use_map. Now collect_expr walks the base expression to build the
   full dot-separated prefix and registers it in use_map at the field
   name span, matching the existing "Module.name" entries in def_map.

2. Wrong hover type for HttpServer.req_body (showed Int): type_at was
   searching the entire type_map, which is shared with stdlib
   declarations. Stdlib spans at coincidentally matching line/col
   numbers were polluting hover results. Added file filter so only
   spans from the current user file are considered.

3. Non-exhaustive match had no quickfix: two root causes in
   missing_cases_for_match — (a) same stdlib span contamination in the
   type lookup, now filtered by file; (b) type_ctors_map keys are bare
   type names ("Option") but Tc.pp_ty renders "Option(Int)", causing
   the lookup to always miss. Now extract the TCon constructor name
   directly instead of using pp_ty. Added a fallback so that if AST
   analysis still produces an empty missing list the diagnostic message
   is used to recover at least the one known missing case.

All 129 LSP tests pass; 14 pre-existing REPL JIT failures are unrelated.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(scheduler): Phase 4 lazy stack growth via guard-page SIGSEGV

Each green thread now reserves MARCH_STACK_MAX (1 MiB) + guard page as
PROT_NONE, with only the top MARCH_STACK_INITIAL (4 KiB) made readable/
writable.  A SIGSEGV handler (SA_SIGINFO | SA_ONSTACK) catches guard-page
faults, calls mprotect to extend the accessible window downward to cover
the faulting page, then returns — the CPU retries the instruction.

Key details:
- stack_alloc_lazy(): reserves full virtual range, makes top 4 KiB R/W
- march_sigsegv_handler(): checks SEGV_ACCERR, finds current process via
  tl_sched->current, mprotects from fault_page up to stack_base in one call
- setup_alt_stack(): 64 KiB sigaltstack per scheduler thread (sched_loop)
- install_stack_growth_handler(): called once from march_sched_init()
- stack_mmap_base field added to march_proc; stack_base tracks current
  usable bottom (decreases on each growth event)
- Dead process cleanup: munmap(p->stack_mmap_base, p->stack_alloc)
- No pointer fixup — virtual address range is pre-reserved, growth in-place

Tests: test_stack_growth_deep (>4 KiB stack depth), test_stack_growth_many
(50 concurrent deep-recursion processes). All 10 scheduler tests pass.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
docs(errors): elm-style error message audit and improvement plan

Comprehensive audit of all ~232 diagnostics emitted by the March compiler.
Quality distribution: A-grade ~35, B-grade ~40, C-grade ~80, D-grade ~77.

The plan covers:
- Style guide: do/don't with examples, multi-line error format spec
- Every message catalogued with current text, location, and trigger
- Before/after improvements for 36 named messages across all 5 phases
- 5-phase implementation roadmap (P1→P4 by user impact)
- Infrastructure work: centralize rendering, did_you_mean utility,
  suggestion field on diagnostic, error snapshot tests

Highlights:
- Parser errors already Elm-quality (A grade); no changes needed
- Type mismatch, variable-not-found, typed hole already excellent
- Biggest wins: unknown type/constructor (add did-you-mean), non-exhaustive
  match (show missing case as code), all eval builtins (name the function)
- Desugar failwith converted to proper Errors.error (DSG-1)
- Import resolver errors need Elm-style rendering (MAIN-1)

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(lsp): P1.1 typed match stubs + P1.7 enhanced type annotations

P1.1 — Fill Missing Match Arms (Typed Stubs):

P1.7 — Add Type Annotation (Enhanced):

- `type_at_point` helper centralises type_map lookup logic

Tests: +9 new LSP tests (107 total)

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
docs(lsp): LSP Performance Insights plan — Elm-style compiler knowledge as diagnostics

Adds specs/plans/lsp-performance-insights-plan.md (450+ lines), a 3-phase
implementation plan for surfacing the March compiler's optimization decisions
as plain-English, actionable LSP diagnostics — like Elm's compiler errors,
never Haskell academic jargon.

Core design principle: every message talks about CODE and CONCEPTS, never
compiler internals (no RC, FBIP, Perceus, escape analysis, TIR, closure
capture sets, or reference counts in user-facing text).

Six insight categories documented with before/after examples, exact diagnostic
text, severity, implementation complexity, and compiler infrastructure mapping:

1. Tail call optimization — detect non-tail recursive calls, identify the
   blocking operation (arithmetic, constructor wrap), suggest accumulator pattern
2. Memory reuse opportunities — use-count heuristic via refs_map; explain
   "used in two places so March makes a copy" in plain terms
3. Actor message copying — detect non-linear sends that deep-copy vs linear
   sends that transfer ownership zero-copy
4. Direct vs indirect calls — known_call pass output or callee-is-parameter
   heuristic; surface as inlay hints
5. Allocation hotspots — constructors inside recursive function arms
6. Stack vs heap — escape analysis EStackAlloc promotion as positive hints

Priority tiers:
- P1 (AST-level, no new pipeline): TCO detection, actor copy warning, closure size
- P2 (AST heuristics): reuse opportunities, indirect calls, alloc in loops
- P3 (async TIR pipeline): precise stack/heap, exact reuse, confirmed direct calls

Includes: perf_insight type design for Analysis.t, display mechanisms
(diagnostics/inlay hints/hover/code lens), TCO tail-position algorithm,
actor copy detection algorithm, message style guide with do/don't table,
implementation checklist with test targets, and risk assessment.

Also updates specs/todos.md (3 new P2 items for the 3 phases) and
specs/progress.md (feature bullet for the plan).

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(lsp): code actions P1.8, P2.10, P3.4

P1.8 — Assign to `_` (Discard Result)
  New QuickFix alongside the existing unused-binding actions: replaces the
  binding name with just `_` (i.e. `let _ = expr`), discarding the result.
  Differs from the existing "Prefix with `_x`" action which keeps the name.

P2.10 — Remove Unused Import
  - typecheck.ml: warn_unused_imports now emits warning_with_code with
    code "unused_import" so the diagnostic is machine-readable.
  - analysis.ml: "Remove unused import" QuickFix action on unused_import
    diagnostics. Whole-module imports ("Unused import: nothing from X")
    delete the entire import line. Specific-name imports ("Unused import
    `name` from X") use a comma-aware source scan to remove just the name
    from the braces list, falling back to whole-line deletion when the
    context is unambiguous (single name). "unused_import" registered in
    fix_registry.

P3.4 — Introduce / Remove inspect
  - "Wrap with inspect": finds the innermost type_map span at the cursor
    and wraps it as inspect(expr, "label") where label is derived from
    the expression text.
  - "Remove inspect": scans backwards from cursor for "inspect(" on the
    current line, counts parens to find the matching close, extracts the
    first argument, and replaces the whole call with the inner expression.

+9 tests (107 total in lsp/test/test_lsp.ml); all passing.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
docs(lsp): world-class LSP code actions plan

Research-backed plan covering 31 new/enhanced code actions inspired by
rust-analyzer, OCaml Merlin, Gleam LSP, Elixir RefactorEx, HLS Wingman,
and TypeScript LSP. Organized into 5 implementation phases, P1–P3 priority
tiers, with March-specific innovations for actor boilerplate, session type
scaffolding, and linear type consumption auditing.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(runtime): zero-copy HTTP response builder (section 4.5)

Implements the Response Pipeline from the green-thread scheduler spec:

- runtime/march_http_response.h + .c: new response builder module
  - Pre-serialized status lines for 14 common HTTP codes stored as
    const globals; iovec entries point directly into them (no copy).
  - `march_response_t` struct with 160-slot fixed iovec array.
  - API: init / set_status / add_header / add_date_header / set_body / send.
  - `_Thread_local` 16 KB scratch buffer for dynamic parts (CL digits,
    unknown status codes) — zero malloc on the hot path.
  - Double-buffered Date header cache refreshed ≤ once/second:
    atomic fast-path for the common case, mutex slow-path for refresh.
  - `march_response_send_plaintext(fd)`: TechEmpower /plaintext fast path
    sends "Hello, World!" in 4 iovecs (static headers | cached Date |
    CRLF | body) with zero per-request allocation.
  - `march_http_response_module_init()` pre-seeds the Date cache at
    server startup.

- runtime/march_http.c: include march_http_response.h; call
  march_http_response_module_init() in march_http_server_listen()
  before the accept loop begins.

- test/test_http_response.c: 20 C tests (76 checks) covering:
  init/reset, static status lines (200/404), unknown-code scratch
  formatting (418), header iovec layout, Date cache freshness and
  thread-safety (8 threads × 1000 iters), empty body, and full
  socket-pair round-trips for 200, 404, and plaintext fast-path.

- test/dune: build rule for test_http_response_runner.

SIMD parser integration confirmed: march_http.c already includes
march_http_parse_simd.h and routes all incoming requests through
march_http_parse_request_simd() when MARCH_HTTP_USE_SIMD is defined
(the default); no additional wiring needed.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(sched): Phase 4 reduction counting in compiled LLVM code

- Add thread-local march_tls_reductions + march_yield_from_compiled() to C runtime
- Emit inline load/decrement/store/icmp/br reduction check at every non-leaf
  function prologue and TCO loop header in llvm_emit.ml
- Leaf function optimization: skip check for functions whose bodies only call
  builtins or march_-prefixed C-runtime functions
- Budget: 4000 reductions per quantum (MARCH_REDUCTION_BUDGET)
- 4 new phase4_reduction_codegen IR tests (1092 total; 14 pre-existing failures)
- Update specs/todos.md and specs/progress.md

author: Matthew Gilliam
message: fix(runtime): include march_http_parse_simd.c and march_scheduler.c in compiler link step

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(runtime): SIMD-accelerated HTTP/1.x parser

Add runtime/march_http_parse_simd.c + .h: a standalone C HTTP/1.x
request parser with an SSE4.2 PCMPESTRI fast path and a scalar fallback.

Fast path (x86-64 with -msse4.2):
- Four SIMD helper functions with __attribute__((target("sse4.2")))
- simd_find_space, simd_find_cr, simd_find_colon_or_cr each scan 16
  bytes per PCMPESTRI instruction, dramatically reducing delimiter
  search cost on HTTP-parsing-bound workloads
- All SIMD code guarded by #if defined(__SSE4_2__)

Scalar fallback:
- Always compiled in; used on ARM64 (Apple Silicon) or any non-x86-64
  target — -msse4.2 is accepted without error on ARM (just a warning,
  suppressed with -Wno-unused-command-line-argument in the dune rule)

Clean C API (no March heap dependencies):
- march_http_parse_request_simd(buf, len, &req) → bytes consumed (>0),
  0 for incomplete, -1 for malformed
- march_http_parse_pipelined(buf, len, reqs, max, &consumed) → count
- march_http_simd_available() → 1 if SSE4.2 path compiled in
- march_http_request_t holds zero-copy slices into the caller's buffer
- Supports up to MARCH_HTTP_MAX_HEADERS (64) headers per request

Integration into march_http.c:
- Includes march_http_parse_simd.h
- MARCH_HTTP_USE_SIMD defined by default; suppress with
  -DMARCH_HTTP_DISABLE_SIMD to fall back to the legacy scalar path
- march_http_parse_request() now delegates to the SIMD parser and
  converts C string slices to March heap objects (march_string_lit)
- Legacy scalar path retained verbatim inside #else block

Tests (test/test_http_simd.c — 20 tests, 71 assertions):
- GET minimal, GET multi-header, POST with body
- HTTP/1.0, all HTTP methods (DELETE/PUT/PATCH/OPTIONS/HEAD)
- Pipelined: two requests, max_reqs cap, empty buffer
- Malformed: bad version, invalid method char, empty path
- Partial: truncated headers, truncated request line
- Edge cases: empty input, NULL input, header whitespace trimming,
  20 headers at once, header limit enforcement
- TechEmpower plaintext benchmark pattern
- Informational SIMD availability report

Dune rule added to test/dune; also syncs test_scheduler_mt.c,
march_deque.h, and DMARCH_NUM_SCHEDULERS=1 flag from main.

Update specs/todos.md and specs/progress.md.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix(codegen): ESeq/dec_rc drops call result in ELet bindings

Perceus borrow inference generates post-call decrements as:
  ELet($t, ESeq(call, EDecRC(borrowed_arg)), body)

ESeq(e1, e2) discards e1 and returns e2. Since EDecRC returns
("i64", "0"), the binding $t was set to null instead of the call
result — causing wrong output (binary_trees check=0) and SIGSEGV
in tree traversals that rely on the result.

Fix: when e2 is a pure side-effect expression (EDecRC/EIncRC/
EAtomicDecRC/EAtomicIncRC), return e1's result and emit e2 for
side effects only. These ops produce no meaningful value.

Also add bench/BENCHMARK_REPORT.md with full run results:
- fib(40): 263.5ms (was 287.7ms, −8.4%)
- binary_trees: 238.6ms (was 265.4ms, −10.1%)
- All 29 optimization unit tests pass (borrow, known_call,
  struct_fusion, escape, mutual_tco)
- Open: tree_transform/list_ops SIGSEGV at large inputs,
  dataframe_bench ctor_info collision — filed for follow-up

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(runtime): Phase 1 green thread scheduler

Adds a cooperative M:N green-thread scheduler (N=1 for Phase 1) to the
C runtime:

- runtime/march_scheduler.h — public API: march_proc struct (PID, stack,
  status, reduction counter, mailbox stub, priority, ucontext_t), scheduler
  struct, and six public functions.

- runtime/march_scheduler.c — implementation:
  • mmap'd stacks with PROT_NONE guard pages (sysconf(_SC_PAGE_SIZE))
  • ucontext_t / makecontext / swapcontext for stackful coroutines
  • 64-bit proc pointer passed to trampoline as two int32 halves
  • FIFO run queue; round-robin scheduling; MARCH_REDUCTION_BUDGET=4000
  • march_sched_spawn / march_sched_yield / march_sched_tick / march_sched_run

- test/test_scheduler.c — four tests (all pass):
  1. test_spawn_1000: 1000 processes → counter == 1000
  2. test_yield_interleaving: explicit yield produces A→B→A→B ordering
  3. test_reduction_preemption: march_sched_tick() causes automatic yield
  4. test_nested_spawn: processes can spawn child processes (55 total)

- test/dune — adds (rule) + (alias runtest) to compile and run the C test
  via %{cc} as part of dune runtest.

- specs/plans/green-thread-scheduler.md — implementation plan for all four
  phases (Phase 1 done; Phases 2–4 described for future work).

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(tir): implement borrow inference pass (P7)

lib/tir/borrow.ml — new pre-Perceus analysis pass that infers which
TCon/TString/TPtr function parameters are "borrowed" (only read via
pattern match or field access, never stored, returned, or passed to
owning positions).  Inter-procedural fixpoint: params passed only to
other borrowed positions remain borrowed.

lib/tir/perceus.ml — integrated borrow_map into RC insertion:
  (a) Call sites skip EIncRC for borrowed args that are still live
      after the call (the callee will not consume them).
  (b) Borrowed params are added to the callee's ~borrowed live-at-exit
      set, suppressing scrutinee EDecRC inside the callee.
  (c) Borrowed last-use args (not live after call) get caller-side
      EDecRC after the call instead of callee-side dec.

HTTP Conn middleware pattern (multiple read-only middleware fns) now
generates zero RC ops for the Conn value.  All existing perceus,
atomic_rc, escape, and fusion tests continue to pass.

10 new tests in borrow_inference group:
  - read-only param is borrowed (analysis)
  - returned / stored param is owned (analysis)
  - Int param not borrowed (no RC needed)
  - inter-procedural: stays borrowed / becomes owned
  - no IncRC at call site for borrowed arg (integration)
  - no DecRC in callee for borrowed param (integration)
  - owned param still gets RC (regression guard)
  - HTTP Conn middleware pattern (end-to-end)

specs/optimizations.md, specs/todos.md, specs/progress.md updated.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(opt): known-call optimization + struct update fusion for HTTP performance

## Known-call optimization (lib/tir/known_call.ml)

After Defun, closures are heap-allocated structs and every call site becomes
ECallPtr (indirect dispatch through a function pointer in field 0). This pass
tracks variables bound to a specific EAlloc/EStackAlloc of a $Clo_* struct and
replaces ECallPtr(AVar clo, args) with EApp(apply_fn, clo :: args) — a direct
call to the lifted apply function.

Runs between Defun and Perceus so the (pure) apply functions are still
inlinable. Also included in the Opt fixed-point loop to catch cases revealed
after Perceus/Escape. Inline size threshold raised 15 → 50 to cover medium-
sized HTTP helpers.

## Struct update fusion (lib/tir/fusion.ml:run_struct)

Detects chains of single-use EUpdate operations and merges them:
  let v2 = {v1 | f1=a}; let v3 = {v2 | f2=b}  →  let v3 = {v1 | f1=a; f2=b}
Later writes win on field conflicts. Handles N-step chains via the fixed-point
loop. Eliminates intermediate Conn copies in HTTP middleware pipelines.

## Wiring

- opt.ml coordinator: Known_call.run + Fusion.run_struct added to pass list
- bin/main.ml: Known_call.run between Defun and Perceus (when --opt)
- lib/tir/dune: added known_call module

## Tests (8 new)

- known_call × 4: direct, unknown_unchanged, two_closures, stack_alloc
- struct_fusion × 4: two_updates, three_updates, field_override, no_fuse_multi_use

## Specs

- specs/optimizations.md: P2 marked ✅, struct fusion entry added (§7b)
- specs/todos.md / specs/progress.md: updated counts and feature list

Add escape_module pipeline helper (Lower→Mono→Defun→Perceus→Escape)
and 6 tests covering the core escape analysis invariants:

- locally-discarded EAlloc promoted to EStackAlloc

- value stored as field of another allocation stays EAlloc

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(runtime): zero-copy HTTP response via writev() + sendfile() for static files

- Add march_http_send_response(): builds an iovec array pointing directly at
  header name/value data and the body's march_string buffer, then calls
  writev() in a single syscall — no intermediate heap copy.

- Add march_http_send_file(): sends headers via writev() then transfers the
  file body using sendfile() (Linux) / sendfile() (macOS) directly from the
  kernel page cache. Falls back to a read/write loop on other platforms.
  Content-Type is inferred from the file extension.

- Update connection_thread to call march_http_send_response() directly,
  removing the old serialize-then-send_all two-step path.

- march_http_serialize_response() is kept for callers that need a heap buffer.

- Update march_http.h with the two new public API declarations.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message: Merge branch 'claude/vigorous-wescoff' — TCO as loop transformation in LLVM IR

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(codegen): implement TCO as loop transformation in LLVM IR

Tail-recursive functions no longer generate recursive `call` instructions
in the LLVM IR.  Instead, self-tail-calls are rewritten into a back-edge
loop:

  entry: alloca + store params  →  br label %tco_loop
  tco_loop:
    <function body>
    ; at each tail self-call site:
    store new_arg1, ptr %p1.addr   ; update param slots
    store new_arg2, ptr %p2.addr
    br label %tco_loop             ; back-edge (no call)
    tco_cont:                      ; dead block (structurally valid)

LLVM's mem2reg + DCE eliminate the dead blocks and promote allocas to
registers, producing tight loop code equivalent to a while loop.

Changes:
- lib/tir/llvm_emit.ml:
  - Add tco_fn_name / tco_loop_label / tco_param_info fields to ctx
  - Add has_self_tail_call: traverses TIR tail positions to detect
    self-recursion (ELet body, ESeq e2, ECase branches/default)
  - emit_fn: detect TCO eligibility; if eligible, emit entry→loop bridge,
    set ctx.tco_fn_name, clear after body
  - New EApp guard before the general call case: when tco_fn_name matches,
    evaluate args, store into param alloca slots, br label %tco_loop,
    open dead block, return dummy value

- test/test_march.ml:
  - Add tco_codegen test suite (4 tests):
    factorial (2-param acc) — loop emitted + back-edge present
    fold over list — loop emitted + back-edge present
    non-tail fib — no loop (correctly skipped)
    countdown (1-param) — loop emitted + back-edge present

- specs/design.md: add "Tail-Call Optimization (TCO)" section under
  Compilation Target explaining the loop transformation, its scope
  (self-recursion only), and interaction with Perceus RC operations

- specs/progress.md: update test count (1046→1050), add TCO to feature list
- specs/todos.md: add TCO to Done section

Correctness verified: factorial(10,1)=3628800, factorial(20,1)=2432902008176640000.
list_ops benchmark (fold over 1M elements) produces correct output.
Zero new test failures; pre-existing JIT/REPL failures unchanged.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix(codegen): add int_and/int_or/int_xor/int_not/int_shl/int_shr/int_popcount to JIT codegen

Two root causes for the `%int_and.addr` undefined value error:

1. defun.ml: Missing from `builtin_names` caused these builtins to be
   incorrectly captured as free variables in closures. Any lambda using
   int_and (e.g. hamt/set key_hash) would allocate a closure struct
   with an `int_and` field, then emit_atom would try to load from
   `%int_and.addr` — which never existed.

2. llvm_emit.ml: No inline EApp case for these ops meant direct calls
   would emit `call i64 @int_and(...)` against an undeclared symbol.

Fix: add all 7 bitwise builtins to:
- defun.ml builtin_names (prevents erroneous free-variable capture)
- llvm_emit.ml is_builtin_fn (skips RC operations)
- llvm_emit.ml builtin_ret_ty (returns TInt for all)
- llvm_emit.ml EApp inline cases (emit LLVM and/or/xor/shl/ashr/ctpop)

Also adds llvm.ctpop.i64 intrinsic declaration to the preamble and a
parity test suite for bitwise builtins (interp vs JIT).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix(tce): refine TCE to allow structural recursion

The mandatory tail-call enforcement pass was rejecting valid structural
recursion (fib, tree-map, binary-trees) that is guaranteed to terminate
because arguments shrink at every recursive call.

Structural recursion rules (non-tail calls now allowed when):
- Argument is a pattern-bound sub-component of a parameter
  (e.g. l, r from Node(l, r) on a param) — detected by collecting
  vars from match arm patterns when the scrutinee is a parameter
- Argument is an arithmetic reduction of a parameter
  (e.g. n-1, n-2) — detected by recognising `param - expr` form

Truly unbounded non-tail recursion (same argument, no reduction)
still emits an error.

Implementation:
- collect_pattern_vars: collect all vars bound by a pattern
- is_structurally_smaller: checks pattern-bound or arithmetic reduction
- scrutinee_is_param_or_smaller: drives smaller-var extension in match
- check_tail_position: now takes fn_params + threads `smaller` set
  through match arms; errors only for genuinely unbounded recursion
- enforce_tail_calls_in_decls: extracts fn param names from fc_params

Test changes:
- test_tc_tail_factorial_fail: updated to use n*factorial(n) (no reduction)
- test_tc_tail_map_fail: updated to use map(xs,f) (same arg)
- test_tc_tail_mutual_fail: updated to use pong(n) (no reduction)
- test_tc_tail_match_arms_fail: updated to use sum_list(xs) (same arg)
- 5 new structural recursion tests: fib n-1+n-2, tree make d-1,
  tree map pattern substructure, sum_list pattern-bound t, infloop fail

Fixes fib.march, binary_trees.march, tree_transform.march benchmarks.

message:
feat(types): type-level natural number constraint solver v1

  Vec(2+3, Int) parses as Vec(TAdd(TNat 2, TNat 3), Int).

Typechecker:
- normalize_nat: folds concrete arithmetic (2+3→5), identity (0+n→n),
  annihilation (0*n→0), and nested ops recursively.
- TVar binding: normalizes nat expressions before the occurs check so
  that 0+r trivially reduces to r before testing for recursion.
- Nat unification: normalizes both sides, then checks concrete equality,
  structural recursion on TNatOp, or single-variable solving (n+2=5→n=3).
  Multiple unknowns emit "Cannot solve type-level constraint: ... (multiple unknowns)".
- Error messages: "Type-level size mismatch: 3 ≠ 5".

arithmetic, identity/annihilation, concat signature, solving, and errors.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix(jit): defer compiled_fns marking until after successful compile+dlopen

Root cause: partition_fns eagerly added functions to compiled_fns before
compile_fragment was called.  If compilation (clang or dlopen) failed,
those functions were poisoned — marked compiled but absent from any .so.
On the next REPL expression they were treated as externs (declared but
not defined), causing cascading "undefined symbol" errors for ALL stdlib
functions used in that session.

Fix:
- partition_fns is now a pure classification function (no side effects)
- New mark_compiled_fns(ctx, fns) called ONLY after compile_fragment
  + dlopen succeeds in run_expr and run_decl

This ensures failed compilations never corrupt the compiled-function
registry, so the next REPL expression gets a clean retry.

Tests: 3 new regression tests in test_march.ml:
  - test_repl_jit_stdlib_reverse (List.reverse across 2 fragments)
  - test_repl_jit_stdlib_no_precompile (inline-JIT mode, 3 calls)
  - test_repl_jit_stdlib_length_3x (List.length in 3 successive frags)

All 815 tests pass.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(typecheck,stdlib): SRec unfolding + Set/BigInt/Decimal/Iterable

Typecheck:
- Add unfold_srec helper that expands SRec(x, body) by substituting
  SVar(x) with the full SRec node, enabling recursive session types.
  Apply it in all five Chan handlers (send/recv/close/choose/offer)
  so protocol loops work correctly without falling through to errors.

Stdlib:
- stdlib/set.march: AVL-tree backed Set(a) with empty, insert, contains,
  remove, union, intersection, difference, size, to_list, from_list, eq.
- stdlib/bigint.march: Arbitrary-precision integers using base-10000
  little-endian digit lists. from_int, add, sub, mul, div, mod_,
  compare, eq, to_string, abs, is_zero. Implements Eq, Ord, Show.
- stdlib/decimal.march: Fixed-point decimal backed by BigInt + scale.
  from_string, from_int, add, sub, mul, div, round, to_string, compare,
  eq, abs, is_zero. Implements Eq, Ord, Show.
- stdlib/iterable.march: Fleshed out from 28-line placeholder into a
  concrete List-based module: map, filter, fold, take, drop, zip,
  enumerate, flat_map, any, all, find, count.
- bin/main.ml: Register all four new modules in the stdlib load list.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(typecheck): add actor handler return type gap checks

- Detect duplicate handler names: two `on Msg(...)` arms for the same
  message name now emit a clear error pointing to the duplicate

- Fix constructor arity for unannotated params: previously
  `List.filter_map param_ty` dropped params without type annotations,
  registering the constructor with 0 args and silently accepting wrong-
  arity sends.  Now `List.mapi` includes every param; unannotated ones
  get a unique `TyVar "$p<i>_<Msg>"` placeholder so `send(pid, Msg(x))`
  typechecks correctly regardless of whether the handler annotates `x`.

- Same arity fix applied to both `check_decl` (pass-2) and the
  `check_module` pass-1 pre-binding loop so forward references are
  consistent.

- 9 new typecheck tests: duplicate-name error, wrong return type,
  init wrong type, multiple-handlers-all-correct, one-wrong-among-many,
  unannotated-param correct/wrong arity, state-spread, no-param msgs.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(typecheck): add pattern match exhaustiveness checking

Implements a Maranget-style exhaustiveness checker for match expressions.
Non-exhaustive matches now emit Warning diagnostics with a missing-case example.

What's covered:
- ADTs: verifies all constructors covered; handles wildcards and variables
- Bool: true + false must both appear (or a wildcard)
- Nested patterns: Some(Some(x)) / Some(None) / None checked recursively
- Tuples: multi-column matrix algorithm for (A, B) patterns
- Int/String/Float: requires a wildcard catch-all
- Guards: check is skipped when any arm has a guard (undecidable)
- As-patterns, atom patterns, record patterns handled conservatively

Algorithm: pattern matrix specialization with the `find_missing_mc` function
that returns a counterexample value when the match is non-exhaustive.

Adds 25 new tests covering all the above cases.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(lsp): add documentation display to hover, completions, and stdlib

- Add `doc_map` to `Analysis.t` — extracts `fn_doc` triple-quoted doc
  strings from the AST and indexes them by name (bare and qualified).
- `collect_docs` walks all decls including nested modules so stdlib
  functions like `List.filter_map` are accessible as both `filter_map`
  and `List.filter_map`.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(parser): multi-error recovery via restart-from-boundary loop

Adds Parse_state (error buffer) and Recovery (restart loop) modules to
march_parser. The parser retains its original raise-on-error behaviour;
Recovery wraps it in a loop that records each error, scans the source for
the next declaration-start keyword, and re-parses from there, accumulating
declarations across retries. Errors are capped at 20 to prevent cascading
noise.

- lib/parser/parse_state.ml: mutable error buffer (init/add/get)
- lib/parser/recovery.ml: line_table, next_decl_boundary, try_parse_from,
  parse_recovering; initial full-source parse is direct (no header
  injection); recovery sub-parses wrap the fragment in "mod Name do\n"
  with corrected pos_lnum so reported positions match the original file
- lib/errors/errors.ml: render_parse_error_at_pos for post-parse rendering
- bin/main.ml: replace direct Parser.module_ call with Recovery.parse_recovering
- lsp/lib/analysis.ml: same; parse errors become LSP diagnostics
- test/test_march.ml: 6 new multi-error recovery tests (658 total, all pass)

  and injects register_supervisor() call after actor EAlloc for supervised actors
- Add link/unlink/register_supervisor builtins to runtime header and
  llvm_emit declarations

Tests: 17 new tests covering

- actor_tir_lowering: type/function generation, dispatch ECase, branch count,
  spawn TPtr, handler params, state loading, EAlloc, supervision registration,

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(lsp): add march-lsp Language Server Protocol server

Adds a full LSP server for the March language in lsp/ using linol/linol-lwt.
The server reuses the march compiler libraries directly (no reimplementation)
so types and diagnostics are always accurate.

Features:
- Diagnostics: parse + typecheck errors/warnings/hints with spans
- Hover: show inferred type at cursor (full HM type_map from typechecker)
- Go-to-definition: def_map (name → span) + use_map (use-span → name)
- Completion: keywords, variables, type constructors, interfaces
- Inlay hints: inferred types inline for let-bindings
- Document symbols: outline of functions, types, interfaces, modules
- Semantic tokens: rich syntax classification beyond keyword patterns
- Actor info: hover on actor names shows state fields + message handlers
- Code actions: stub ready for linear type suggestions and sort hints

Also removes the old hand-rolled march_lsp stub from bin/dune (kept the
.ml as reference) and adds the march-lsp package to dune-project.

Architecture:
  lsp/lib/position.ml  — span ↔ LSP position utilities
  lsp/lib/analysis.ml  — document analysis cache (parse→tc→collect)
  lsp/lib/server.ml    — linol server class with all handlers
  lsp/bin/main.ml      — stdio JSON-RPC entry point

feat(syntax): change match syntax from 'with' to 'do' (Elixir case-style)

Breaking change: `match expr with | Pat -> body end` is now
`match expr do | Pat -> body end`.

  leading `|` via option(PIPE) inside the branch rule

  for unambiguous re-parsing

  count_token "do"), simplifies do_end_depth to count_token "do" minus
  count_token "end"

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(fmt): add march fmt formatter — Phase 2 tooling

Implements the March source code formatter per specs/plans/tooling-plan.md:

- lib/format/format.ml: AST-based pretty-printer (march_format library)
  - 2-space indentation, 80-char soft line-width target

    lambdas, actors, interfaces, impl, extern, protocol, app

    boundaries using line-number matching against the original source

  - --fmt flag  — format source before compiling

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
feat(test): add reference interpreter oracle

Adds test/test_oracle.ml — a standalone executable that runs every
.march file in bench/ and examples/ through both the tree-walking
interpreter and the native compiler, then compares stdout.

Discovered bugs (first pass):
  - 7 programs MATCH interpreter == compiled
  - 4 MISMATCH: Pid representation (Pid(0) vs #<tag:0>),
    drop handler ordering, and typecheck warnings going to stdout
  - 10 RUN_FAIL(crashed): sort benchmarks + list_lib segfault
  - 0 COMPILE_FAIL: compiler succeeds on all programs

Wire-up:
  dune build @test/oracle   # runs the oracle and prints matrix
  dune exec test/test_oracle.exe --root .   # direct invocation

The oracle uses deterministic output paths (stable across runs) so
the march CAS stays valid. Signal-killed binaries are reported as
RUN_FAIL(crashed). Network/server programs are skipped; heavy
benchmarks show INTERP_TIMEOUT (expected).

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix(repl): help text, actor multiline, and continuation detection

Bug 2: :help in run_simple REPL (non-TUI, non-debug) was silently
swallowed — the arm had a spurious `when is_debug` guard.  Now both
modes print appropriate help text.

Bug 3: `count_match_with` counted any line ending with the word "with",
including record-update expressions like `{ state with`.  Those don't
need a balancing `end`, so they inflated `do_end_depth` and the REPL
never submitted the actor declaration.  Fix: only count lines that also
contain `match` (genuine `match expr with` openers).

Bug 4: `is_complete` had no detection for continuation tokens that
signal an incomplete expression: trailing `=` (let/fn binding), `->`,
`then`, or `else`.  Add `ends_with_continuation` and wire it into
`is_complete` so `let result =<newline>42` works as expected.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix(repl): restore syntax highlighting for comments and live input

Comments (-- ...) were silently discarded by the lexer with no token
emitted, causing comment-only lines to render as empty images and
inline comments to be swallowed entirely.

Live input (make_input_img) used A.empty for all text, so the buffer
appeared flat gray as the user typed.

Fixes:
- highlight.ml: add find_comment_start to detect -- not inside strings,
  add attr_comment (muted blue-gray), split input at comment boundary
  and render comment suffix with attr_comment via the new public
  highlight function (internal tokeniser renamed to highlight_tokens)
- repl.ml: make_input_img now calls Highlight.highlight on left/right
  segments around the cursor block instead of I.string A.empty

message:
feat(modules) pub/sig enforcement — Phase 1 visibility + Phase 2 sig conformance

Phase 1: pub/private visibility

  bare forms produce Private

Phase 2: sig conformance with type checking

  constructors are filtered out of new_ctors

Examples updated: modules.march, modules_nested.march, modules_private.march
now use `pub fn`/`pub mod` for public APIs and bare `fn` for private impl.

9 new tests covering pub/private fn, mod, let, type and sig conformance.
All 607 tests pass.

author: Matthew Gilliam
message:
fix(jit): uniquify REPL globals as repl_N_name to prevent redefinition collision

REPL globals were named "@repl_<bare>" (e.g. @repl_y), so redefining a
variable produced two LLVM declarations in the same fragment — one external
(from prev_globals) and one new global — causing clang to fail with:

  error: redefinition of global '@repl_y'

Fix: rename globals to "repl_<N>_<bare>" where N is the fragment number
(e.g. @repl_5_y), guaranteeing each fragment defines a fresh symbol with
no collision against prior fragments.  bare_of_global in both llvm_emit.ml
and repl_jit.ml is updated to strip the "repl_N_" prefix.  ctx.globals is
deduplicated by bare name on update so emit_prev_global_bridges never
allocates two allocas for the same variable after a redefinition.
